blocks:
Surah Hud

Meccan, and it is 123 verses.

Surah Hud (peace be upon him)

Meccan according to the saying of most (scholars), and Qatadah said (it is Meccan) except for one verse, which is His saying: وَأَقِمِ الصَّلاةَ طَرَفَيِ النَّهارِ (And maintain the prayer at the two ends of the day) for it is Medinan. The number of its verses is one hundred and twenty-three verses. بِسْمِ اللهِ الرَّحْمنِ الرَّحِيمِ (In the Name of Allah, the All-beneficent, the All-merciful)

(١) الر (Alif, Lām, Rā) Its interpretation was previously mentioned at the beginning of Surah Yunus. كِتابٌ أُحْكِمَتْ آياتُهُ (a Book whose signs have been made firm) It has been arranged in a firm arrangement (نظماً محكماً); there is no deficiency in it, nor any flaw, like a firmly constructed building (كالبناء المحكم). ثُمَّ فُصِّلَتْ (then elaborated) with the proofs of Monotheism (التوحيد), the admonitions (المواعظ), the rulings (الأحكام), and the stories (القِصَص). And the meaning of ثُمَّ (then) (1) is delay in state (التراخي في الحال), not in time (لا في الوقت). Al-Qummi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It is the Quran. مِنْ لَدُنْ حَكِيمٍ خَبِيرٍ (from One who is Wise, All-aware) He said: from the presence of One who is Wise, All-aware.

(٢) أَلَّا تَعْبُدُوا إِلَّا اللهَ إِنَّنِي لَكُمْ مِنْهُ نَذِيرٌ وَبَشِيرٌ ** (2) that you shall not worship anyone but Allah. Indeed, I am for you a warner and a bearer of good news from Him. من الله (from Allah) نَذِيرٌ وَبَشِيرٌ ** (a warner and a bearer of good news) with punishment for al-shirk (الشِّرك) and reward for al-tawhid (التوحيد). (٣) وَأَنِ اسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّكُمْ ** (3) And that you seek forgiveness of your Lord, from al-shirk (الشِّرك) and disobedience (المعصية), ثُمَّ تُوبُوا إِلَيْهِ ** (then turn to Him penitently) through faith (الإيمان) and obedience (الطاعة). يُمَتِّعْكُمْ مَتاعاً حَسَناً ** (He will provide you with a good provision) He will cause you to live (2) in security (أمن) and tranquility (ودعة) إِلى أَجَلٍ مُسَمًّى ** (until a specified term) which is the end of your decreed lifespans, وَيُؤْتِ كُلَّ ذِي فَضْلٍ فَضْلَهُ (and He will grant His grace to every meritorious person) and He will give every meritorious person in his religion the reward (جزاء) for his merit (فضله) in this world (الدنيا) and the Hereafter (الآخِرة). وَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا ** (But if you turn away) And if you turn away فَإِنِّي أَخافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذابَ يَوْمٍ كَبِيرٍ ** (then I fear for you the punishment of a tremendous day) the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) means the smoke (الدّخان) and the cry (الصّيحَة).

(4) إِلَى اللهِ مَرْجِعُكُمْ ** (4) To Allah is your return, on that Day, وَهُوَ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ قَدِيرٌ ** and He has power over all things. so He is able to punish you with the severest punishment. It is as if this is an affirmation of the magnitude of the Day. (5) أَلا إِنَّهُمْ يَثْنُونَ صُدُورَهُمْ ** (5) Look! They fold up their chests they bend them, لِيَسْتَخْفُوا مِنْهُ ** that they may hide from Him. from Allah with their secret, so that His Messenger and the believers may not become aware of it, or [they hide] from His Messenger. In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Jabir ibn Abdullah informed me that when the polytheists passed by the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم around the House (البيت), one of them would lower his back and his head like this, and cover his head with his garment so that the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم would not see him. So Allah revealed the Ayah. And al-Qummi [says]: They conceal what is in their chests (صدورهم) of hatred for Ali (peace be upon him). The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Indeed, the sign (آية) of the hypocrite is hatred for Ali." And there were people who displayed affection for Ali in the presence of the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم while concealing their hatred for him. In al-Jawami' [it is mentioned]: And in the reading of the Household (أهل البيت), [it is read as] يَثْنَوْني (yathnawnī), based on the pattern يفعوعل (yafʿawʿal) derived from الثَّني (al-thany), which is a structure of hyperbole (بناء مبالغة). أَلا حِينَ يَسْتَغْشُونَ ثِيابَهُمْ ** Look! When they cover themselves with their garments, they cover themselves with their garments out of dislike for hearing the speech of Allah, like His Almighty saying: جَعَلُوا أَصابِعَهُمْ فِي آذانِهِمْ وَاسْتَغْشَوْا ثِيابَهُمْ يَعْلَمُ ما يُسِرُّونَ ** (They put their fingers in their ears and covered themselves with their garments.) He knows what they keep secret in their hearts, وَما يُعْلِنُونَ ** and what they disclose. with their mouths. Their secret and their public acts are equal in His knowledge. إِنَّهُ عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ ** Indeed, He is aware of what is in the breasts. [meaning] the secrets of the breasts (ذات الصدور) or the hearts (القلوب) and their conditions. It was said that it was revealed concerning a group of polytheists who said: "If we lower our curtains, cover ourselves with our garments, and conceal in our chests (صدورنا) hatred for Muhammad, how will He know?" And al-Qummi [says]: When the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم spoke about something concerning the virtue (فضل) of Ali, or recited to them what Allah had revealed concerning him, they would shake off their garments and then stand up. Allah says: يَعْلَمُ ما يُسِرُّونَ وَما يُعْلِنُونَ ** (He knows what they keep secret and what they disclose) when they stood up. Indeed, Allah عَلِيمٌ بِذاتِ الصُّدُورِ.** (is aware of what is in the breasts.)

(٦) وَما مِنْ دَابَّةٍ فِي الْأَرْضِ إِلَّا عَلَى اللهِ رِزْقُها ** (There is no animal on the earth but that its provision lies with Allah) due to His undertaking of it (the provision) out of grace and mercy. وَيَعْلَمُ مُسْتَقَرَّها ** (and He knows its permanent abode) the place of its settlement and its dwelling, وَمُسْتَوْدَعَها ** (and its temporary lodging) before settlement, [such as] in the loins of fathers, the wombs of mothers, and eggs. كُلٌ ** (All) every single one of the creatures, its provision, its permanent abode (مُسْتَقَرَّها), and its temporary lodging (مُسْتَوْدَعَها), فِي كِتابٍ مُبِينٍ ** (are in a manifest Book) are mentioned in the Preserved Tablet (اللّوح المحفوظ). In Nahj al-Balagha: He divided their provisions, enumerated their traces and their deeds, the number of their breaths, the treachery of their eyes (خائنة أعينهم), what their hearts conceal of conscience (الضمير), their permanent abode (مُسْتَقَرَّهُم), and their temporary lodging (مُسْتَوْدَعَهُم)—from the wombs (الأرحام) and the loins (الظهور)—until they reach their ultimate ends (الغايات).

(٧) وَهُوَ الَّذِي خَلَقَ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضَ فِي سِتَّةِ أَيَّامٍ ** (7) (It is He who created the heavens and the earth in six days,) meaning, He created them both and what is between them, as its explanation preceded in Surat al-A'raf. وَكانَ عَرْشُهُ عَلَى الْماءِ ** (and His Throne was upon the water,) before He created them both. Al-Qummi said: And that was at the beginning (مَبْدَأ) of creation.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, originated all things by His knowledge, without a prior example (مِثال) that existed before it. So He originated the heavens and the earths, and before them, there were neither heavens nor earths. Do you not hear His Almighty saying: وَكانَ عَرْشُهُ عَلَى الْماءِ. (and His Throne was upon the water,)

And in it (al-Kafi) and in al-Tawhid, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَكانَ عَرْشُهُ عَلَى الْماءِ ** (and His Throne was upon the water,). He asked: "What do they say?" It was said: "They say that the Throne was upon the water, and the Lord is above it." He said: "They have lied. Whoever claims this has made Allah carried (مَحْمُولاً) and described Him with the attribute (صِفَة) of created beings, and it necessarily follows that the thing that carries Him is stronger than Him." Then he said: "Indeed, Allah carried His religion (دِين) and His knowledge (عِلْم) upon the water before there was a heaven, or an earth, or jinn, or humans, or a sun, or a moon."

And in the narration of al-Qummi: And the water was upon the air (الهواء), and the air is boundless (لا يُحَدّ). And on that day, nothing was created besides the two of them, and the water was sweet and palatable (عَذْب فُرات).

I say: The interpretation (تَأْوِيل) of these narrations is known to those firmly grounded in knowledge (الرّاسِخين في العِلم). لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ أَيُّكُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلاً ** (that He may test you [to see] which of you is best in conduct.) meaning, He created them for a profound wisdom (حِكْمَة بالِغَة), which is that He might make them dwellings (مَساكِن) for His servants, and bestow upon them therein various kinds (فُنُون) of blessings, and charge them with duties (يُكَلِّفَهُم), and expose them to the reward (ثَواب) of the Hereafter. And since this resembles the testing of the tester, He said: لِيَبْلُوَكُمْ ** (that He may test you), meaning: that He may do with you what the tester does regarding your states—how you act. And since testing contains the meaning of knowledge (العِلْم), and it is a path to it, He said: أَيُّكُمْ أَحْسَنُ عَمَلاً. (which of you is best in conduct.)

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He does not mean the one among you who has the most deeds (أَكْثَرُكُمْ عَمَلاً), but rather the one among you whose deeds are most correct (أَصْوَبُكُمْ عَمَلاً). And correctness (الإِصابَة) is only the fear of Allah (خَشْيَة الله) and sincere intention (النّيّة الصادقة). And the general populace (العامّة) narrated from the Prophet (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace): Which of you is best in intellect (أَحْسَنُ عَقْلاً), most abstinent from the forbidden things of Allah (أَوْرَعُ عَن مَحارِم الله), and swiftest in obedience to Allah (طاعَة الله).

وَلَئِنْ قُلْتَ إِنَّكُمْ مَبْعُوثُونَ مِنْ بَعْدِ الْمَوْتِ ** (And if you say, 'You will indeed be raised after death,') so that they may anticipate it, لَيَقُولَنَّ الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا إِنْ هذا إِلَّا سِحْرٌ مُبِينٌ ** (the faithless will surely say, 'This is nothing but manifest magic.') meaning, an apparent deception (تَمْوِيه ظاهِر) for which there is no reality (حَقِيقَة). And it was read (سَاحِر) (magician).

(8) وَلَئِنْ أَخَّرْنا عَنْهُمُ الْعَذابَ ** (8) If We defer their punishment— the promised one إِلى أُمَّةٍ مَعْدُودَةٍ ** until a counted term— It was said (قيل) that this refers to a small group (جماعة) of times (الأوقات). And al-Qummi narrates from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): He means by it the time (الوقت). لَيَقُولُنَ ** they will surely say, out of seeking haste (استعجالاً) and mockery (واستهزاءً): ما يَحْبِسُهُ ** 'What holds it back?' What prevents it from occurring? أَلا يَوْمَ يَأْتِيهِمْ لَيْسَ مَصْرُوفاً عَنْهُمْ ** Beware! The day it overtakes them, it will not be averted from them, The punishment will not be repelled (مدفوعاً) from them, and it will encompass them (وَحاقَ بِهِمْ) and surround them (وأحاط بهم). The past tense (الماضي) was placed in the position of the future tense (المستقبل) for confirmation (تحقيقاً) and exaggeration (مبالغة) in the threat (التهديد). ما كانُوا بِهِ يَسْتَهْزِؤُنَ. and they will be besieged by what they used to ridicule. Al-Qummi means that if We grant them enjoyment (متّعناهم) in this world (هذه الدنيا) until the emergence (خروج) of the Qā'im (peace be upon him), then We will bring them back (نردّهم) and punish them (ونعذّبهم). لَيَقُولُنَّ ما يَحْبِسُهُ ** meaning they will say, "Will the Qā'im not rise? Will He not emerge?" in the manner of mockery (على حدّ الاستهزاءِ). And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): The counted term (الأمّة المعدودة) is the companions (أصحابُ) of the Qā'im: the three hundred and ten-some men (الثلاثمأة والبضعة عشر). And al-Ayyashi narrates from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that He said: It is the Qā'im and His companions. And narrated from Him (peace be upon him): إِلى أُمَّةٍ مَعْدُودَةٍ ** means a number (عدة) like the number of [men at] Badr (كعدّة بدر). لَيْسَ مَصْرُوفاً عَنْهُمْ ** He said: the punishment (العذاب). And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The companions of the Qā'im, the three hundred and ten-some men (الثلاثمائة والبضعة عشر رجلاً), they are, by Allah, the counted term (الأمّة المعدودة) which Allah mentioned in His Book. He recited this verse and said: By Allah, they will gather in a single hour, scattered (قزعاً) (1) like the scattered clouds (كقزع الخريف) of autumn. And in al-Kāfī and al-Majma': that which is close to it.

(٩) وَلَئِنْ أَذَقْنَا الْإِنْسانَ مِنَّا رَحْمَةً ثُمَّ نَزَعْناها ** (9) And if We let man taste a mercy from Us, and then withdraw it from him, We took it away مِنْهُ إِنَّهُ لَيَؤُسٌ ** from him, he is indeed despairing, intensely despairing (شديد اليأس), hopeless (قنوط) that that withdrawn blessing (النعمة المنزوعة) will return to him, cutting off his hope from the vastness of Allah's favor, extremely ungrateful (كَفُورٌ), great in ingratitude (عظيم الكفران) for His blessings.

footnote (١). القزع محرّكة قطع من السّحاب رقيقة والواحد قزعة وفي الحديث : كأنّهم قزع الخريف ص. (1). Al-Qaza' (القزع), with the ḥarakah (vowel marks), are thin pieces of clouds, and the singular is qaz'ah (قزعة). And in the Hadith: "It is as if they are the qaza' (قزع) of autumn." ص.

(١٠) وَلَئِنْ أَذَقْناهُ نَعْماءَ بَعْدَ ضَرَّاءَ مَسَّتْهُ ** (10) And if We let him taste bliss after a distress that has befallen him, like health after sickness, and wealth after poverty (عُدْم). And in the difference between the two verbs in attribution (الإسناد) there is a subtle point (نكتَة) that is not hidden. لَيَقُولَنَّ ذَهَبَ السَّيِّئاتُ عَنِّي ** he will surely say, 'The evils have left me.' meaning the calamities (المصائب) that distressed me and saddened me. He is indeed joyful (فَرِحٌ), insolent (اشِر), boastful (بطر), deceived (مغتّر) by it, and proud (فَخُورٌ) over people because of what Allah has bestowed upon him. Joy and pride have distracted him from giving thanks (الشكر) and fulfilling its right.

Al-Qummi said: If Allah enriches the servant and then he becomes poor, despair (اليأس), anxiety (الجزع), and panic (الهلع) afflict him (1). And if Allah removes that from him, he rejoices. It was said regarding the two terms, tasting (الإذاقة) and touching (المسّ), that they serve as an alert that what man finds in this world of blessings (النعم) and trials (المحن) is like a sample (كالانموذج) of what he will find in the Hereafter, and that he falls into ingratitude (الكفران) and insolence (البطر) due to the slightest thing, because tasting (الذوق) is the perception of flavor, and touching (المسّ) is the beginning of arrival (الوصُول).

(١١) إِلَّا الَّذِينَ صَبَرُوا (Except those who are patient) (They are patient) in hardship upon affliction, as faith in Allah and submission to His decree,

وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ (and perform righteous deeds) in ease, as gratitude for His bounties, both their preceding and their succeeding.

أُولئِكَ لَهُمْ مَغْفِرَةٌ وَأَجْرٌ كَبِيرٌ. (For such there will be forgiveness and a great reward.)

(١٢) **فَلَعَلَّكَ تارِكٌ بَعْضَ ما يُوحى إِلَيْكَ ** (12) Maybe you will omit some of what is revealed to you,

You omit the conveyance of some of what is revealed to you, which is that which contradicts the opinion of the polytheists, fearing their rejection and mockery of it, وَضائِقٌ بِهِ صَدْرُكَ (and your breast is constrained by it) lest they say: "Why has not a treasure been sent down to him?" which he might spend on gaining followers, like kings, أَوْ جاءَ مَعَهُ مَلَكٌ ** (or an angel come with him?) who would confirm him. إِنَّما أَنْتَ نَذِيرٌ ** (You are only a warner). You are only responsible for warning with what has been revealed to you, and it is not incumbent upon you whether they reject or propose suggestions. So what is the matter with you that your breast is constrained by it? وَاللهُ عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ وَكِيلٌ ** (and Allah is guardian over all things.) So rely upon Him, for He is aware of their condition and will deal with them according to the recompense of their sayings and actions. Discussion concerning this verse will come in Surat Bani Isra'il, if Allah wills.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: When the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم stopped at Qudayd [or Ghadir, variant reading], He said to Ali (peace be upon him): "I asked my Lord to establish guardianship (الولاية) between me and you, and He did so. I asked my Lord to establish brotherhood (المؤاخاة) between me and you, and He did so. I asked my Lord to make you my successor (وصيّي), and He did so." Then two men from Quraysh said: "By Allah, a sa' (صاع) of dates in a worn-out waterskin (شن بالٍ) is dearer to us than what Muhammad صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم asked his Lord. Why did He not ask his Lord for an angel to support Him against His enemy, or a treasure by which He could dispense with His poverty? By Allah, He never called upon Him for truth or falsehood except that Allah answered Him." So Allah sent down to Him: **فَلَعَلَّكَ تارِكٌ ** (Maybe you will omit...) the verse.

And al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated something close to this. Al-Ayyashi added: The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم prayed for the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) at the end of His prayer, raising His voice so that the people could hear, saying: "O Allah, grant Ali affection (المودّة) in the hearts of the believers, and awe and greatness (الهيبة والعظمة) in the hearts of the hypocrites." Then the Almighty Allah sent down: إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ سَيَجْعَلُ لَهُمُ الرَّحْمنُ وُدًّا ** (Indeed, those who have faith and do righteous deeds—the All-beneficent will vest love in them.) Then "Ma'..." said: "By Allah, a sa' (صاع) of dates in a worn-out waterskin (شنّ بال) is dearer to me than what Muhammad asked his Lord. Why did He not ask Him for an angel to support Him, or a treasure by which He could gain strength against His poverty?" So Allah sent down concerning him ten verses from [Surah] Hud, the first of which is: فَلَعَلَّكَ تارِكٌ بَعْضَ ما يُوحى إِلَيْكَ ** (Maybe you will omit some of what is revealed to you) the verse.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Zayd ibn Arqam, who said: Indeed, Gabriel, the Trustworthy Spirit (الرّوح الأمين), descended upon the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم with the guardianship (ولاية) of Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him) on the evening of Arafah. The Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم felt constrained by this, fearing the denial of the people of slander (الإفك) and hypocrisy (النّفاق). So He called a group of people, among whom I was, and consulted them about proclaiming this during the pilgrimage season (الموسم). We did not know what to say to Him, and He wept. Then Gabriel said to Him: "O Muhammad, have you become impatient (أجزعت) with the command of Allah?" He replied: "No, O Gabriel, but my Lord already knows what I have endured from Quraysh, as they did not acknowledge my prophethood (الرسالة) until He commanded me to fight them and sent down armies from the heaven to help me. How then will they acknowledge Ali (peace be upon him) after me?" Then Gabriel departed from Him, and [the verse] descended upon Him: فَلَعَلَّكَ تارِكٌ **بَعْضَ ما ** (Maybe you will omit some of what...) the verse.

(1) Al-halaʿ (الهلع), with a fatḥah on the ḥā’ and lām, is the worst form of impatience (الجزع). (ق)

أَمْ يَقُولُونَ افْتَراهُ قُلْ فَأْتُوا بِعَشْرِ سُوَرٍ (١) (Or do they say, 'He has fabricated it'? Say, 'Then bring ten fabricated surahs like it) مِثْلِهِ ** (like it) in eloquence (البيان) and excellence of composition (حسن النظم). مُفْتَرَياتٍ ** (fabricated) invented (مختلقات) from your own selves, if it is true that I fabricated it from my own self. For you are eloquent Arabs (عرب فصحاء) like me; you are capable of the like of what I am capable of. Rather, you are more capable due to your knowledge of stories (القصص) and your habituation to poetry (الأشعار). وَادْعُوا مَنِ اسْتَطَعْتُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ صادِقِينَ (and call on whomever you can, besides Allah, if you are truthful.') to assist in the challenge (المعارضة), if you are truthful that it is fabricated (مفتر).

footnote (١) وهذا صريح في التحدّي وفيه دلالة على جهة إعجاز القرآن وإنّما هي البلاغة والفصاحة في هذا النّظم المخصوص لأنّه لو كان جهة الإعجاز غير ذلك لما قنع في المعارضة بالافتراء والاختلاق لأنّ البلاغة ثلاث طبقات فأعلى طبقاتها معجز وأدناها وأوسطها ممكن فالتحدّي في الآية انّما وقع في الطّبقة العليا منها ولو كان وجه الإعجاز الصّرف لكان الرّكيك من الكلام أبلغ من باب الإعجاز والمثل المذكور في الآية لا يجوز أن يكون المراد به مثله في الجنس لأنّ مثله في الجنس يكون حكاية فلا يقع بها التحدّي وانّما يرجع ذلك الى ما هو متعارف بين العرب في تحدّي بعضهم بعضاً كما اشتهر في مناقضات امرئ القيس وعلقمة وغيرها م ن. (1) And this is explicit in the challenge (التحدّي). In it is an indication of the aspect of the miraculousness (إعجاز) of the Quran, which is only the eloquence (البلاغة) and fluency (الفصاحة) in this specific composition (النظم المخصوص). Because if the aspect of miraculousness were other than that, He would not have been satisfied with fabrication (الافتراء) and invention (الاختلاق) in the challenge (المعارضة). Because eloquence (البلاغة) has three levels (طبقات): the highest of its levels is miraculous (معجز), and the lowest and middle are possible (ممكن). Thus, the challenge (التحدّي) in the Ayah only occurred regarding the highest level of it. And if the aspect of miraculousness were purely (الصرف) the composition (النظم), then the weak (الركيك) speech would be more eloquent (أبلغ) from the perspective of miraculousness (الإعجاز). And the likeness (المثل) mentioned in the Ayah cannot be intended to mean its likeness in kind (الجنس), because its likeness in kind would be a mere narration (حكاية), and the challenge (التحدّي) would not be established by it. Rather, that refers back to what is customary among the Arabs in challenging one another, as became famous in the refutations (مناقضات) of Imru' al-Qays (امرئ القيس) and 'Alqamah (علقمة) and others. (م ن).

(١٤) فَإِلَّمْ يَسْتَجِيبُوا لَكُمْ ** (14) If they do not respond to you, O believers, those whom you invited to the challenge (المعارضة), or O disbelievers, those whom you invited to assistance (المعاونة), فَاعْلَمُوا أَنَّما أُنْزِلَ بِعِلْمِ اللهِ ** then know that it has been sent down with Allah’s knowledge, being intertwined with that which none knows except Allah, and none has power over except Him, وَأَنْ لا إِلهَ إِلَّا هُوَ ** and that there is no god except Him. And know that there is no god except Him, because He is the All-Knowing (العالم), the All-Powerful (القادر) regarding that which none other than Him knows or has power over, due to the manifest inability (عجز) of those who were called upon (المدعوّين). فَهَلْ أَنْتُمْ مُسْلِمُونَ ** Will you then submit? [Meaning:] Are you firm (ثابتون) upon Islam, rooted (راسخون) in it, or entering (داخلون) into Islam, sincere (مخلصُون) in it?

(١٥) مَنْ كانَ يُرِيدُ الْحَياةَ الدُّنْيا وَزِينَتَها ** (Whoever desires the life of this world and its adornment) through his good deeds and righteousness. Al-Ayyashi narrated from Al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It means so-and-so and so-and-so. نُوَفِّ إِلَيْهِمْ أَعْمالَهُمْ فِيها ** (We will pay them in full the reward of their deeds in it) We deliver to them the recompense of their deeds in the world, such as health, leadership, ample provision, and numerous children. **وَهُمْ فِيها لا يُبْخَسُونَ ** (and they will not be defrauded in it.) They will not be diminished anything from their rewards.

(16) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ لَيْسَ لَهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ إِلَّا النَّارُ ** (16) They are the ones for whom there is nothing in the Hereafter except the Fire. because they have fully received what the forms (صور) of their good deeds necessitated, and the burdens (أوزار) of their evil intentions (العزائم السّيئة) remain upon them. وَحَبِطَ ما صَنَعُوا فِيها ** (and what they did therein has failed.) meaning, in the Hereafter, because they do not desire it, and it is inherently futile (باطِلٌ في نفسه) ما كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ ** (what they used to do.) because it was not performed as it ought to be, and no reward (ثواب) remains for it in the Hereafter. It is also permissible to connect (تعليق) the word فيها (therein) to صَنَعُوا (they did) and refer the pronoun (الضمير) back to the world (الدنيا).

Al-Qummi said: It means that whoever performs a good deed intending that Allah grant him its reward (ثوابه) in the world (الدنيا), Allah grants him its reward in the world, and he shall have nothing فِي الْآخِرَةِ إِلَّا النَّارُ. (in the Hereafter except the Fire.)

(١٧) أَفَمَنْ كانَ عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّهِ ** (Is he who stands on a manifest proof from his Lord) [Meaning] upon a clear proof (برهان) from Allah that guides him to the truth (الحق) and correctness (الصواب) in what he undertakes and what he leaves. And the hamzah is for denial, [denying] that one whose status is such should follow these individuals whose aspirations and thoughts are limited to the worldly life, or that they should be considered close in rank. Meaning: أَفَمَنْ كانَ عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ ** (Is he who stands on a manifest proof) [like] one who desires the worldly life? How can this be, when there is a vast difference (بون بعيد) between them? وَيَتْلُوهُ شاهِدٌ مِنْهُ (and whom a witness from Him follows). He is followed by شاهِدٌ ** (a witness) who testifies for him, [coming] from Him. وَمِنْ قَبْلِهِ كِتابُ مُوسى إِماماً وَرَحْمَةً. (and before him was the Book of Moses, a guide and a mercy.) Meaning the Torah (التّورية).

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Kazim and Imam al-Rida (peace be upon them both): The Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him) is the Witness (الشاهد) over the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, and the Messenger of Allah is عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّهِ. (on a manifest proof from his Lord).

In al-Majma', narrated from the Commander of the Faithful, Imam al-Baqir, and Imam al-Rida (peace be upon them): That the Witness (الشاهد) from Him is Ali ibn Abi Talib (peace be upon him), who testifies for the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, and he is from him.

And al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed thus: أَفَمَنْ كانَ عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّهِ وَيَتْلُوهُ شاهِدٌ مِنْهُ إِماماً وَرَحْمَةً وَمِنْ قَبْلِهِ كِتابُ مُوسى ** (Is he who stands on a manifest proof from his Lord, and whom a witness from Him follows, a guide and a mercy, and before him was the Book of Moses). And narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It was only revealed thus: أَفَمَنْ كانَ عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّهِ ** (Is he who stands on a manifest proof from his Lord), meaning the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, وَيَتْلُوهُ ** (and whom follows him) is Ali, شاهِدٌ مِنْهُ وَمِنْ قَبْلِهِ كِتابُ مُوسى إِماماً وَرَحْمَةً أُولئِكَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ ** (a witness from Him, and before him was the Book of Moses, a guide and a mercy. These believe in Him). So, they advanced and delayed [words] in the composition (التأليف).

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The one who is عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّهِ ** (on a manifest proof from his Lord) is the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, and the one who followed him thereafter, the Witness (الشاهد) from Him, is the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), then his successors (أوصياؤه), one after the other.

And narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): There is no man from Quraysh except that an Ayah or two Ayahs from the Book of Allah have been revealed concerning him. A man from the people asked: "Then what was revealed concerning you, O Commander of the Faithful?" He replied: "Do you not read the Ayah which is in [Surah] Hud: أَفَمَنْ كانَ عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّهِ وَيَتْلُوهُ شاهِدٌ مِنْهُ ** (Is he who stands on a manifest proof from his Lord, and whom a witness from Him follows)? Muhammad is on a manifest proof from his Lord, and I am the Witness (الشاهد)." And in al-Amali and al-Basa'ir: similar [to this]. And in al-Amali: "And I am the Witness (الشاهد) and I am from him." And in al-Basa'ir: "And I am a witness for him concerning it, and I follow him with it."

I say: Based on this narration, the intent of the manifest proof (البيّنة) is the Quran, and يَتْلُوهُ ** (follows him) is derived from recitation (التّلاوة).

And in al-Ihtijaj: That he (peace be upon him) was asked about his greatest merit (منقبة), so he recited this Ayah and said: "I am the Witness (الشاهد) from the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم." And in it (al-Ihtijaj), in a hadith: Some of the heretics (الزّنادقة) said to him: "I find that Allah informs that a شاهِدٌ مِنْهُ ** (witness from Him) follows His Prophet, yet the one who followed him was a worshipper of idols (عبدة الأصنام) for a period of his life." He (peace be upon him) replied: "As for His saying وَيَتْلُوهُ شاهِدٌ مِنْهُ (and whom a witness from Him follows), that is the Proof (حجّة) of Allah, which Allah established over His creation, and He informed them that no one deserves the position (مجلس) of the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم except one who stands in his place, and no one follows him except one who is like him in purity (الطهارة) and rank (منزلته), lest the defilement (رجس) of disbelief (الكفر) touch one who falsely claims the right to the station of the Messenger at any time, and to restrict the excuse for anyone who assists him in his sin (إثمه) and oppression (ظلمه). This is because Allah forbade anyone touched by disbelief (الكفر) from assuming what He entrusted to His Prophets and His Saints (أوليائه), by His Almighty saying to Abraham: لا يَنالُ عَهْدِي الظَّالِمِينَ (My covenant shall not reach the wrongdoers), meaning the polytheists (المشركين), because He called polytheism (الشّرك) oppression (ظلماً) in His saying: إِنَّ الشِّرْكَ لَظُلْمٌ عَظِيمٌ (Indeed polytheism is a great wrong). When Abraham knew that the covenant of Allah would not reach the idol worshippers (عبدة الأصنام), he said: وَاجْنُبْنِي وَبَنِيَّ أَنْ نَعْبُدَ الْأَصْنامَ ** (Keep me and my children away from worshipping idols). And know that whoever prefers the hypocrites (المنافقين) over the truthful (الصادقين) and the disbelievers (الكفّار) over the righteous (الأبرار) has certainly fabricated a great sin (إثماً عظيماً) against Allah, since He has clarified in His Book the difference between the one who is right (المحق) and the one who is false (المبطل), the pure (الطاهر) and the impure (النّجس), the believer (المؤمن) and the disbeliever (الكافر), and that no one follows the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم in his covenant upon his passing except one who occupies his place in truthfulness (صدقاً), justice (عدلاً), purity (طهارة), and merit (فضلاً)."

And in al-Majma', narrated from al-Husayn ibn Ali (peace be upon him): The Witness (الشاهد) from Allah is Muhammad.

I say: Based on this, فَمَنْ كانَ عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ ** (Is he who stands on a manifest proof) encompasses every sincere believer (مؤمن مخلص) who possesses insight (بصيرة) in his religion. This does not contradict its revelation concerning the Prophet and the Successor (الوَصيِّ). The view of generalization is held by those who interpreted the Witness (الشاهد) as the Quran, meaning a witness from Allah that testifies to its authenticity. أُولئِكَ يُؤْمِنُونَ بِهِ ** (These believe in Him), [meaning] in the Quran or in the Messenger. وَمَنْ يَكْفُرْ بِهِ مِنَ الْأَحْزابِ ** (But whoever disbelieves in Him from among the factions), [meaning] from the people of Mecca and those who formed factions (تحزب) with them against the Messenger of Allah صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم, فَالنَّارُ مَوْعِدُهُ ** (the Fire is his tryst). He shall inevitably enter it.

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: No one from this nation (الأمة) hears of me—neither a Jew nor a Christian—and then does not believe in me, except that he will be among the inhabitants of the Fire. فَلا تَكُ فِي مِرْيَةٍ مِنْهُ ** (So do not be in doubt about it), [meaning] concerning the Quran or the tryst (الموعد).

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [The meaning of the Ayah is] concerning the guardianship (ولاية) of Ali (peace be upon him): إِنَّهُ الْحَقُّ مِنْ (It is the truth from) رَبِّكَ وَلكِنَّ أَكْثَرَ النَّاسِ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ. (your Lord, but most people do not believe.)

(١٨) وَمَنْ أَظْلَمُ مِمَّنِ افْتَرى عَلَى اللهِ كَذِباً أُولئِكَ يُعْرَضُونَ عَلى رَبِّهِمْ وَيَقُولُ الْأَشْهادُ هؤُلاءِ الَّذِينَ كَذَبُوا عَلى رَبِّهِمْ أَلا لَعْنَةُ اللهِ عَلَى الظَّالِمِينَ. (18) Who is a greater wrongdoer than him who fabricates a lie against Allah? Such shall be presented before their Lord, and the witnesses will say, 'These are the ones who lied against their Lord.' Behold! The curse of Allah is on the wrongdoers.

(١٩) الَّذِينَ يَصُدُّونَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** (Those who bar [others] from the way of Allah) from His religion وَيَبْغُونَها عِوَجاً ** (and seek to make it crooked) they seek deviation (زيغاً) from uprightness (الاستقامة) for the way of Allah; they distort it through interpretation (بالتأويل) or they seek it through deviation (بالانحراف) from the truth (الحق) and correctness (الصواب). وَهُمْ بِالْآخِرَةِ هُمْ كافِرُونَ. (and they are faithless to the Hereafter.) Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They are four kings from Quraysh, following one another. I say: The four kings are the three [Caliphs] and Mu'awiyah. And narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): الْأَشْهادُ ** (the witnesses) are the Imams (peace be upon them). Al-Qummi said: By the witnesses (الإشهاد), He means the Imams (peace be upon them). أَلا لَعْنَةُ اللهِ عَلَى الظَّالِمِينَ ** (Behold! The curse of Allah is on the wrongdoers) [who usurped] the right (حقهم) of the Family of Muhammad (آل محمّد). يَصُدُّونَ عَنْ سَبِيلِ اللهِ ** (Those who bar [others] from the way of Allah) [means] from the path of Allah (طريق الله), which is the Imamate (الإمامة). يَبْغُونَها عِوَجاً ** (and seek to make it crooked) [means] they diverted it (حرّفوها) to something else (إِلى غيرها).

(٢٠) أُولئِكَ لَمْ يَكُونُوا مُعْجِزِينَ فِي الْأَرْضِ ** (20) These will not be able to frustrate [Him] on the earth, They were not able to frustrate Allah in the world (الدنيا) from punishing them. وَما كانَ لَهُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ مِنْ أَوْلِياءَ and they had no guardians besides Allah who could prevent them from the punishment had He willed to punish them. However, He postponed their punishment until this Day (اليوم) so that it might be more severe (أشدّ) and more lasting (أدوم). يُضاعَفُ لَهُمُ الْعَذابُ The punishment will be doubled for them. ما كانُوا يَسْتَطِيعُونَ السَّمْعَ They could not hear, due to their deafness (لتصامّهم) toward the truth (الحق) and their hatred (بغضهم) for it. Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: They were not able to hear the mention of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him). وَما كانُوا يُبْصِرُونَ and they could not see, due to their blindness (لتعاميهم) toward the signs (آيات) of Allah.

(٢١) أُولئِكَ الَّذِينَ خَسِرُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ وَضَلَّ عَنْهُمْ ما كانُوا يَفْتَرُونَ ** (21) They are the ones who have ruined their souls, and what they used to fabricate has failed them. They lost what they expended, and what they acquired was lost from them, so nothing remained with them except regret (الحَسرة) and remorse (الندامَة). Al-Qummi said: [It refers to] those who called upon others besides the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him).

(٢٢) لا جَرَمَ أَنَّهُمْ فِي الْآخِرَةِ هُمُ الْأَخْسَرُونَ ** (22) There is no doubt that they will be the greatest losers in the Hereafter. No one is clearer and more in loss than them. (٢٣) إِنَّ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا وَعَمِلُوا الصَّالِحاتِ وَأَخْبَتُوا إِلى رَبِّهِمْ ** (23) Indeed, those who have faith and do righteous deeds and humble themselves before their Lord— They were tranquil toward Him and humbled themselves before Him. أُولئِكَ أَصْحابُ الْجَنَّةِ هُمْ فِيهَا خالِدُونَ. they are the inhabitants of paradise, and they will remain in it [forever]. (٢٤) مَثَلُ الْفَرِيقَيْنِ ** (24) The parable of the two groups The disbeliever and the believer كَالْأَعْمى وَالْأَصَمِ ** is that of the blind and the deaf, Like the blind and the deaf, or like the blind and deaf [combined]. وَالْبَصِيرِ وَالسَّمِيعِ ** and the seeing and the hearing. Like the seeing and the hearing, or like the seeing and hearing [combined]. That is because of the disbeliever's blindness (تعامي) toward the signs of Allah, his deafness (تصامه) toward listening to the speech of Allah, and his refusal (تأبيّه) to reflect upon its meanings. هَلْ يَسْتَوِيانِ مَثَلاً أَفَلا تَذَكَّرُونَ ** Are they equal in comparison? Will you not then take admonition? By the striking of parables and reflecting upon them.

(٢٥) وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا نُوحاً إِلى قَوْمِهِ إِنِّي لَكُمْ that I am for you وقرئ (And it was recited) with kasrah (الكسر) نَذِيرٌ مُبِينٌ making clear to you the causes (موجبات) of the punishment and the way to salvation (وجه الخلاص). (25) Certainly We sent Noah to his people. He said, 'Indeed I am a manifest warner to you.'

(٢٦) أَنْ لا تَعْبُدُوا إِلَّا اللهَ إِنِّي أَخافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذابَ يَوْمٍ أَلِيمٍ painful (مؤلم). The mention of Noah's name, lineage (نسبه), law (شريعته), and the glad tidings concerning him has already preceded in Surat al-A'raf. (26) 'that you shall not worship anyone but Allah. Indeed, I fear for you the punishment of a painful day.'

(٢٧) فَقالَ الْمَلَأُ The nobles (الاشراف) الَّذِينَ كَفَرُوا مِنْ قَوْمِهِ ما نَراكَ إِلَّا بَشَراً مِثْلَنا meaning: you have no distinction (لا مزيّة) over us that would single you out for prophethood (النبوة) and the necessity of obedience (وجوب الطاعة). وَما نَراكَ اتَّبَعَكَ إِلَّا الَّذِينَ هُمْ أَراذِلُنا our meanest ones (أخسّاؤنا). Al-Qummi (narrated): meaning the poor (الفقراء) and the needy (المساكين). بادِيَ الرَّأْيِ meaning: the apparent opinion (ظاهر الرأي) without deep consideration (من غير تعمق), derived from البدو (appearing), or the first opinion (أوّل الرأي) derived from البَدء (beginning). They only considered them low (استرذلوهم) because of their poverty, for since they knew nothing except the apparent aspect (ظاهراً) of the worldly life (الحيوة الدنيا), the one who was most fortunate in it was considered nobler (أشرف) by them, and the deprived one was considered lower (أرذل). وَما نَرى لَكُمْ for you (Noah) and your followers (ولمتّبعيك) عَلَيْنا مِنْ فَضْلٍ that would qualify you (يؤهّلكم) for prophethood (للنّبوة) and the right to be followed (استحقاق المتابعة). بَلْ نَظُنُّكُمْ كاذِبِينَ you (Noah) in the claim of prophethood (دعوى النبوّة) and they (your followers) in the claim of knowing your truthfulness (دعوى العلم بصدقِك). (27) Then the chiefs who were faithless among his people said, 'We do not see you but as a human like ourselves, and we do not see anyone following you except those who are the lowest among us, at first glance, and we do not see that you have any advantage over us. Rather, we think you are liars.'

(٢٨) قالَ يا قَوْمِ أَرَأَيْتُمْ ** (28) He said, 'O my people, do you see?' Inform me (أخبروني) إِنْ كُنْتُ عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّي ** if I stand on a manifest proof from my Lord, a proof (حجّة) testifying to the truth of my claim (دعواي), وَآتانِي رَحْمَةً مِنْ عِنْدِهِ and He has given me a mercy from Him, by giving the manifest proof (البيّنة) or prophethood (النّبوة), فَعُمِّيَتْ عَلَيْكُمْ ** and it has been obscured from you, so it was hidden from you (فخفيت عليكم) and became ambiguous (واشتبهت), such that you did not recognize it (لم تعرفوها) and did not understand it (ولم تفهموها), and thus it did not guide you (فلم تهدكم). And it was read with the dammah on the ayn and the tashdid on the mim (وقرى بضم العين وتشديد الميم). أَنُلْزِمُكُمُوها should we compel you to it? Should we force you to be guided by it (أنكرهكم على الاهتداء بها) وَأَنْتُمْ لَها كارِهُونَ while you are averse to it? You do not choose it (لا تختارونها) and you do not reflect upon it (ولا تتأمّلون فيها).

(٢٩) وَيا قَوْمِ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ ** (29) And O my people! I do not ask you for any reward for it على التبليغ مالاً ** for the conveyance [of the message] wealth جعلاً إِنْ أَجرِيَ إِلَّا عَلَى اللهِ ** as a wage/fee My reward is only with Allah فانّه المأمول منه وَما أَنَا بِطارِدِ الَّذِينَ آمَنُوا for He is the One from Whom it is hoped. And I will not drive away those who have faith يعني الفقراء وهو جواب لهم حين سألوا طردهم إِنَّهُمْ مُلاقُوا رَبِّهِمْ ** meaning the poor ones, and this is a response to them when they requested their expulsion. Indeed, they are going to meet their Lord يلاقونه ويفوزون بقربه فيخاصمون طاردهم فكيف أطردهم وَلكِنِّي أَراكُمْ قَوْماً تَجْهَلُونَ ** They will meet Him and attain success through His proximity, and they will dispute with the one who expelled them, so how can I expel them? But I see that you are a people who are ignorant الحق وأهله أو تتسفّهونَ عليهم بأن تدعوهم أراذل. of the truth and its people, or you act foolishly/lightly towards them by calling them the lowest (أراذل).

(٣٠) وَيا قَوْمِ مَنْ يَنْصُرُنِي مِنَ اللهِ ** (30) O my people! Who will help me against Allah, by repelling His vengeance إِنْ طَرَدْتُهُمْ ** (if I drive them away)? while they are in that high station أَفَلا تَذَكَّرُونَ ** (Will you not take admonition,) so that you may know that seeking their expulsion and making faith conditional upon it is not correct. (٣١) وَلا أَقُولُ لَكُمْ عِنْدِي خَزائِنُ اللهِ ** (31) I do not say to you that I have the treasuries of Allah, the treasuries of His provision, such that you deny my virtue وَلا أَعْلَمُ الْغَيْبَ ** (nor do I know the unseen,) nor do I say that I know the unseen, such that you would deny me out of improbability, or such that I would know that these people followed me based on initial opinion (بادي الرّأي) without insight (بصيرة) and firm conviction (عقد قلب) وَلا أَقُولُ إِنِّي مَلَكٌ ** (nor do I say that I am an angel.) so that you would say: 'You are nothing but a human like us.' وَلا أَقُولُ لِلَّذِينَ تَزْدَرِي أَعْيُنُكُمْ (And I do not say to those whom your eyes despise) Nor do I speak concerning those whom you deem contemptible (استرذلتموهم) due to their poverty, from (زرى عليه) when he found fault with him (عابه). And attributing it to the eyes is for emphasis (للمبالغة) and to draw attention to the fact that you deemed them contemptible based on initial sight (بادي الرؤية) without deliberation (روية) لَنْ يُؤْتِيَهُمُ اللهُ خَيْراً ** (Allah will never grant them any good.) For what Allah has prepared for them in the Hereafter is better than what He grants you in the world. اللهُ أَعْلَمُ بِما فِي أَنْفُسِهِمْ إِنِّي إِذاً لَمِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ ** (Allah knows best what is in their hearts. Indeed, were I to do that, I would be among the wrongdoers.) if I said anything of that.

(٣٢) قالُوا يا نُوحُ قَدْ جادَلْتَنا ** (32) They said, 'O Noah, you have disputed with us, You contended with us (خاصمتنا). فَأَكْثَرْتَ جِدالَنا ** and you have prolonged your dispute with us. So you prolonged it (فأطلته). فَأْتِنا بِما تَعِدُنا ** Now bring us what you promise us, Of the punishment (من العذاب). إِنْ كُنْتَ مِنَ الصَّادِقِينَ ** if you are one of the truthful.' In the claim (الدعوى) and the threat (الوعيد), for your disputation (مناظرتك) does not affect us (لا تؤثر فينا).

(33) قالَ إِنَّما يَأْتِيكُمْ بِهِ اللهُ إِنْ شاءَ ** swiftly or belatedly (عاجلاً أو آجلاً) وَما أَنْتُمْ بِمُعْجِزِينَ ** by repelling the punishment and fleeing from it. (33) He said, 'Only Allah will bring it upon you if He wishes, and you cannot frustrate [Him].'

(34) وَلا يَنْفَعُكُمْ نُصْحِي إِنْ أَرَدْتُ أَنْ أَنْصَحَ لَكُمْ إِنْ كانَ اللهُ يُرِيدُ أَنْ **يُغْوِيَكُمْ ** by knowing from you persistence in disbelief, so He left you to your own devices. (34) My advice will not benefit you, even if I desire to advise you, if Allah desires to lead you astray.

In Qurb al-Isnad and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): It means the matter belongs to Allah the Almighty; He guides whom He wills. And al-Ayyashi added: and leads astray. And al-Ayyashi and al-Qummi, narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): It was revealed concerning al-Abbas.

I say: It means concerning him and those like him, if the revelation is generalized. هُوَ رَبُّكُمْ وَإِلَيْهِ تُرْجَعُونَ. (He is your Lord, and to Him you shall be brought back.)

(٣٥) أَمْ يَقُولُونَ افْتَراهُ ** (This is) an interjection (اعتراض). (35) Or do they say, 'He has fabricated it'? قُلْ إِنِ افْتَرَيْتُهُ فَعَلَيَّ إِجْرامِي ** (meaning) its consequence (وباله). And it was recited with the opening of the hamzah (بفتح الهمزة) in the plural form (على الجمع). Say, 'If I have fabricated it, then my sin is upon me وَأَنَا بَرِيءٌ مِمَّا تُجْرِمُونَ ** (meaning) from your sin (اجرامكم) in attributing the fabrication (الافتراءِ) to me. and I am innocent of what you commit as sins.'

(٣٦) وَأُوحِيَ إِلى نُوحٍ أَنَّهُ لَنْ يُؤْمِنَ مِنْ قَوْمِكَ إِلَّا مَنْ قَدْ آمَنَ فَلا تَبْتَئِسْ (It was revealed to Noah that none of your people will believe except those who have already believed, so do not grieve) So do not grieve the grief of a miserable, submissive person بِما كانُوا يَفْعَلُونَ (because of what they have been doing.) Allah made him despair of their belief and forbade Him from being distressed by what they did of denial and harm (الإيذاء).

في الكافي والعيّاشيّ عن الباقر عليه السلام : أنّ نوحاً لبث في قومه ألفَ سَنة الا خمسين عاماً يدعوهم سرّاً وعلانية فلّما أبوا وعتوا قال ربّ أَنِّي مَغْلُوبٌ فَانْتَصِرْ فأوحى الله تعالى إليه In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Noah remained among his people for nine hundred and fifty years, inviting them secretly and openly. When they refused and rebelled (عَتَوْا), he said: "My Lord, I am overcome, so help me (فَانْتَصِرْ)." So the Almighty Allah revealed to him: أَنَّهُ لَنْ يُؤْمِنَ مِنْ قَوْمِكَ إِلَّا مَنْ قَدْ آمَنَ فَلا تَبْتَئِسْ بِما كانُوا يَفْعَلُونَ (that none of your people will believe except those who have already believed, so do not grieve because of what they have been doing.) For that reason, Noah said: وَلا يَلِدُوا إِلَّا فاجِراً كَفَّاراً. (and do not let them beget anyone but vicious ungrateful disbelievers.)

(٣٧) وَاصْنَعِ الْفُلْكَ بِأَعْيُنِنا ** (37) And construct the Ark under Our eyes [The phrase بِأَعْيُنِنا ** (under Our eyes) means] being accompanied by Our eyes. [The verse] expressed, through the multiplicity of the instrument of sense by which a thing is preserved and guarded against defect (الاختلال) and deviation (الزّيغ), the exaggeration in preservation and care, using the method of representation (التمثيل). وَوَحْيِنا ** (and Our revelation) [means Our revelation] to you how you should construct it. وَلا تُخاطِبْنِي فِي الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ** (And do not address Me concerning those who have done wrong) means do not appeal to Me regarding them, and do not call upon Me seeking to ward off the punishment from them. **إِنَّهُمْ مُغْرَقُونَ ** (Indeed, they shall be drowned) [means] it has been decreed upon them that they shall be drowned, so there is no way to avert it.

(٣٨) وَيَصْنَعُ الْفُلْكَ ** (38) And he built the ark This is a narration of his past state. وَكُلَّما مَرَّ عَلَيْهِ مَلَأٌ مِنْ قَوْمِهِ سَخِرُوا مِنْهُ ** and whenever a company of his people passed by him, they derided him. They mocked him for his construction of the ship. It was said that he was building it in a wilderness far from water, or that he was carving it (نجرته), and they were laughing at him and saying: "You have become a carpenter (نجّاراً) after you were a prophet (نبيّاً)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): When Noah planted the date stones (النّوى), his people passed by him and began to laugh and mock, saying: "He has settled down (١) as a planter (غرّاساً)." Until the date palms grew tall and became mighty (٢) and towering (٣), he cut them down and then carved them (٤). So they said: "He has settled down as a carpenter (نجّاراً)." Then he assembled it and made it a ship. They passed by him and began to laugh and mock, saying: "He has settled down as a sailor (ملّاحاً) in a desolate land (فلاة من الأرض)," until he finished it.

قالَ إِنْ تَسْخَرُوا مِنَّا فَإِنَّا نَسْخَرُ مِنْكُمْ كَما تَسْخَرُونَ ** He said, 'If you deride us, we will deride you just as you deride us. When the drowning overtakes you in this world (الدنيا) and the burning in the Hereafter (الآخرة).

(٣٩) Soon you will know whom a humiliating punishment will befall. He means them by this, and the punishment (العذاب) is the drowning (الغرق), and a lasting, perpetual punishment (عَذابٌ مُقِيمٌ) will descend upon him, which is the punishment of the Fire (عذاب النار). (٤٠) When Our command came and the oven gushed forth [water], (5) The water sprang forth from it and rose up like a boiling pot.

In al-Kafi and al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The oven (التَّنُّورُ) was in the house of a believing old woman, behind the qiblah on the right side of the mosque, meaning the Mosque of Kufa (مسجد الكوفة). He was asked: "That is the location of the corner of Bab al-Fil (باب الفيل) today." Then he was asked: "Was the beginning of the water's emergence from that oven (التّنور)?" He said: "Yes. Indeed, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, loved to show the people of Noah (نوح) a sign (آية). Then Allah Almighty sent the rain pouring forth abundantly, and the Euphrates (الفرات) poured forth abundantly, and all the springs poured forth abundantly, so Allah drowned them and saved Noah (نوح) and those who were with him in the Ark (السفينة)."

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Noah's (نوح) wife came to him while he was building the Ark (السفينة) and said to him: "Water has emerged from the oven (التَّنُّورُ)." So he quickly went to it until he placed the lid upon it and sealed it with his ring (خاتمه), and the water stopped. When he finished the Ark, he came to his ring, broke the seal, and removed the lid, and the water gushed forth.

In al-Kafi, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): When Noah (نوح) finished the Ark (السفينة), the appointed time between him and his Lord for the destruction of his people was for the oven (التَّنُّورُ) to gush forth. So it gushed forth. His wife said: "The oven (التّنور) has gushed forth." So he went to it and sealed it, and the water stopped. He admitted those whom He wished to admit and removed those whom He wished to remove. Then he came to his ring (خاتمه) and removed it. Allah says: So We opened the gates of the sky with pouring water, and We made the earth burst forth with springs, and the waters met for a matter that had been decreed. Its construction (نجرها) was in the middle of your mosque. We said, 'Carry in it (in the Ark) a pair from every kind, meaning, from every category, a male category and a female category. It was also read with tanwin on kull (كُلٍّ), meaning, from every beneficial type of animal, two pairs (زوجين)—a pair, a male and a female. and your family, meaning his wife and his sons and their wives, except those against whom the word has already gone forth that he is among the drowned. This refers to his son Canaan (كَنعان) and his wife Wahilah (واهلة), for they were disbelievers. and those who have faith.' And the believers from others. But none had faith with him except a few.

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Eight people from his people had faith with Noah (نوح).

And in al-Ma'ani, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): The like of it. And al-Qummi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith: When Noah (نوح) finished building the Ark (السفينة), Allah commanded him to call out in Syriac (السّريانيّة) so that no beast (بهيمة) or animal (حيوان) remained except that it attended. So he admitted two pairs (زوجين) of every species (جنس) of animal species into the Ark. And those who believed in him from the entire world were eighty (1) men. So Allah said: 'Carry in it a pair from every kind, (the Ayah). The construction (نجر) of the Ark was in the Mosque of Kufa (مسجد الكوفة). When the day came on which Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, willed their destruction, Noah's wife was giving news in the place known as Far al-Tannur (فار التّنور) in the Mosque of Kufa. Noah (peace be upon him) had prepared a place in the Ark for every type of animal species and gathered for them therein what they needed of sustenance (الغذاء). His wife cried out when the oven (التّنور) gushed forth. So Noah came to the oven, placed clay upon it, and sealed it until he had admitted all the animals into the Ark. Then he came to the oven, broke the seal, and removed the clay. The sun was eclipsed, and pouring water came from the sky—a downpour without drops—and the earth burst forth with springs. This is His saying, the Glorified: So We opened the gates of the sky with pouring water, and We made the earth burst forth with springs, and the waters met for a matter that had been decreed. And narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Not everyone on earth among the Children of Adam (بني آدم) is from the progeny of Noah (نوح) (peace be upon him). Allah Almighty said in His Book: 'Carry in it a pair from every kind, up to His saying: and those who have faith.' And He said: O descendants of those whom We carried with Noah! (peace be upon him).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Noah (نوح) carried in the Ark the eight pairs (الأزواج الثمانية) that Allah mentioned: eight pairs: So there were two of sheep, one pair (زوج) domesticated (داجنة) (1) which people raise, and the other pair (الزّوج الآخر) being the sheep that live in the wild mountains, whose hunting He made lawful for them—the hadith. The completion of this narrative has preceded in Surat al-An'am. And in al-Majma' and al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): When Allah willed the destruction of Noah's (نوح) people (peace be upon him), He made the wombs of the women barren (عقّم أرحام النّساءِ) for forty years, so no child was born to them. When Noah finished building the Ark, Allah commanded him to call out in Syriac (السّريانّية) for all the animals to gather. No animal remained except that it attended. So he admitted two pairs (زوجين) of every species (جنس) of animal species, except for the mouse (الفأر) and the cat (السّنّور). And when they complained about the dung (سرقين) of the beasts and the filth (القذر), he prayed for the pig (الخنزير). He wiped its forehead, and it sneezed, and a pair (زوج) fell from its nose, and they multiplied. When they became numerous, they complained to him about them, so he prayed for the lion (الأسد). He wiped its forehead, and it sneezed, and a pair of cats (زوج سنّور) fell from its nose. And in another hadith: They complained about the excrement (العذرة), so Allah Almighty commanded the elephant (الفيل), and it sneezed, and the pig (الخنزير) fell out.

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Noah (نوح) carried the dog (الكلب) in the Ark but did not carry the child of fornication (ولد الزّنا). And narrated from him (peace be upon him): It is fitting that the testimony of the child of fornication (ولد الزّنا) should not be accepted, nor should he lead the people in prayer (يؤمّ بالناس). Noah did not carry him in the Ark, although he carried the dog (الكلب) and the pig (الخنزير) in it.

And in al-Ilal, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him), from his father, from his forefathers, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon them): He was asked: "Why is the goat (الماعز) short-tailed (معرقبة الذّنب) and its private parts (الحياءِ والعورة) exposed?" He replied: "Because the goat disobeyed Noah (نوح) when he brought her into the Ark, so he pushed her and broke her tail. And the ewe (النعجة) has its private parts covered because the ewe hastened to enter the Ark, so Noah wiped his hand over her private parts and her tail, and the fat tail (الألية) became straight."

And in al-Khisal, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): Noah (نوح) prepared ninety compartments (بيتاً) in the Ark (الفلك) for the beasts (للبّهائم).

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Allah commanded Noah (نوح) to carry in the Ark a pair from every kind, so he carried the male date palm (الفحل) and the Ajwah date palm (العجوة), and they were a pair (زوجاً).

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The length of Noah's (نوح) Ark (peace be upon him) was one thousand two hundred cubits (ذراع), its width was eight hundred cubits, and its height (طولها في السماء) was eighty cubits.

And al-Qummi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The like of it, as will follow. And in al-Uyun, in the Syrian narrative (الخبر الشّامي): The length is mentioned as eight hundred and the width as five hundred.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), regarding the merit of the Mosque of Kufa (مسجد الكوفة): He said: And from it the oven gushed forth [water], and in it the Ark was tarred (بخرت). And the like of it is in al-Majma', narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him). And in a narration in al-Kafi: And from it, it sailed (سارت).

And al-Ayyashi, narrated from Salman, from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him), regarding its merit: In it Noah (نوح) constructed his Ark (سفينته), and in it the oven (التَّنُّورُ) gushed forth, and in it was Noah's house and his mosque.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The dwelling of Noah (نوح) (peace be upon him) and his people was in a village one stage (منزل) away from the Euphrates (الفرات), towards the west of Kufa (الكوفة). Noah (peace be upon him) was a carpenter (نجّاراً), so Allah made Him a Prophet (نبيّاً) and chose Him. Noah (peace be upon him) was the first to build a ship (سفينة) that sailed upon the surface of the water. He said: Noah remained among his people for a thousand years save fifty years, calling them to guidance (الهدى), but they would pass by him and mock him. When he saw that from them, he prayed against them and said: 'My Lord! Do not leave any inhabitant of the faithless on the earth! So Allah revealed to him: "O Noah (نوح), build the Ark (الْفُلْكَ), make it spacious, and hasten its construction under Our supervision and according to Our revelation." So Noah built the Ark in the Mosque of Kufa (مسجد الكوفة) with his own hand, bringing the wood from afar until he finished it. He was asked: "In how long did Noah (peace be upon him) build his Ark until he finished it?" He said: "In two cycles (دورين)." He was asked: "And how long are the two cycles?" He said: "Eighty years." It was said: "But the common people (العامّة) say he built it in five hundred years." He replied: "No, by Allah! How could that be, when Allah says: and according to Our revelation."

I say: The end of the hadith bears two meanings. The first is that something done by the command and teaching of Allah—how could its time extend to such a duration? The second is that al-wahy (الوحي) here may have been interpreted as speed (السّرعة) and haste (العجلة), for it has come with this meaning. It is said al-wahā al-wahā (الوحا الوحا), extended and shortened, meaning: "Hurry! Hurry!" (البدار البدار). The second meaning is more complete in terms of evidence (الاستشهاد).

(41) وَقالَ ارْكَبُوا فِيها ** (41) And he said, 'Embark in it. Become riders in it, just as one rides beasts on land. بِسْمِ اللهِ مَجْراها وَمُرْساها In the Name of Allah shall be its course and its mooring. Naming Allah, saying that, and its meaning is: By Allah is its running (اجراؤها) and its anchoring (ارساؤها). And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): meaning its journey (مسيرها) and its stopping place (موقفها). And it was recited مجريها with a fatḥah on the mīm. إِنَّ رَبِّي لَغَفُورٌ رَحِيمٌ ** Indeed my Lord is all-forgiving, all-merciful.' Meaning: Were it not for His forgiveness for your lapses (لفرطاتكم) and His mercy upon you, He would not have saved you.

(٤٢) وَهِيَ تَجْرِي بِهِمْ فِي مَوْجٍ ** (42) And it sailed with them amidst waves of the deluge (الطوفان) كَالْجِبالِ ** (42) like mountains. Every wave among them was like a mountain in its accumulation and height. In al-Khisaal, narrated from al-Kadhim (peace be upon him), and in al-Uyun, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): When Noah (peace be upon him) boarded the Ark, Allah revealed to Him: "O Noah, if you fear drowning, then say تَهْلِيل (the declaration of Divine Unity) a thousand times, then ask Me for salvation, and I shall save you from drowning, along with those who believe with you." He said: When Noah and those with him settled in the Ark and the sail (القلس) was raised (or القلص - variant reading), the wind raged against them. Noah (peace be upon him) did not feel safe, and the wind hastened him, so he could not manage to say تَهْلِيل a thousand times. So, he said in Syriac (بالسّريانّية): "Hīlūlīyā (هيلولياً) a thousand thousand times, O Mārīyā (يا ماريا), make firm (اتقن)." He said: The sail (القلص) became steady, and the Ark proceeded. Noah (peace be upon him) then said: "A phrase by which Allah saved me from drowning is worthy of never leaving me." He said: So, he engraved upon his ring: لا إله إلّا اللهُ (There is no god except Allah) a thousand times, O Lord, set right (يا ربّ أصلح). In al-Ihtijaj, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him), from the Prophet صلىَّ الله عليه وآله وسلم: When Noah boarded the Ark and feared drowning, he said: "O Allah, I ask You by Muhammad and the Family of Muhammad (آل محمّد) that You save me from drowning." So, Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, saved him. وَنادى نُوحٌ ابْنَهُ ** (42) and Noah called out to his son Canaan (كنعان). Al-Qummi and al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was not his son; rather, he was the son of his wife. This is the dialect of [the tribe of] Tayy (طيّ), who refer to the son of a woman as her son (ابنه), meaning with a fathah on the hā (بفتح الهاء). In al-Majma', narrated from Ali, al-Baqir, and al-Sadiq (peace be upon them): They read it thus. It is also narrated: ابنها (her son), and the pronoun refers to his wife (لامرأته). وَكانَ فِي مَعْزِلٍ ** (42) who was in an isolated place: meaning, a place where he isolated himself away from the vessel يا بُنَيَّ ارْكَبْ مَعَنا ** (42) ‘O my son! Embark with us, in the Ark وَلا تَكُنْ مَعَ الْكافِرِينَ. (42) and do not be with the faithless.’ Al-Qummi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Noah (peace be upon him) looked at his son, who was falling and rising, and said to him: يا بُنَيَّ ارْكَبْ** (O my son! Embark...) [the rest of] the Ayah.

(٤٣) قالَ سَآوِي إِلى جَبَلٍ يَعْصِمُنِي مِنَ الْماءِ ** (He said, 'I shall take refuge on a mountain, which will protect me from the water.') In al-Faqih, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He said when he overlooked al-Najaf: "It is the mountain upon which the son of my ancestor Nuh (peace be upon him) sought refuge." So قالَ سَآوِي إِلى جَبَلٍ يَعْصِمُنِي مِنَ الْماءِ ** (He said, 'I shall take refuge on a mountain, which will protect me from the water.'). Then Allah revealed to it: "O mountain, will anyone seek refuge upon you from Me?" So it sank into the earth and was fragmented towards al-Sham (الشّام). And in al-Ilal (العلل): what is close to this (meaning). قالَ لا عاصِمَ الْيَوْمَ مِنْ أَمْرِ اللهِ إِلَّا مَنْ رَحِمَ ** (He said, 'There is no savior today from Allah's command, except him on whom He has mercy.') — except the Merciful One (الرّاحم), and He is Allah, the Almighty. وَحالَ بَيْنَهُمَا الْمَوْجُ (And the waves intervened between the two of them) — between Nuh and his son — فَكانَ مِنَ الْمُغْرَقِينَ.** (and he was among the drowned.)

(٤٤) وَقِيلَ يا أَرْضُ ابْلَعِي ماءَكِ (11:44) (And it was said, 'O earth, swallow your water,') meaning: Dry up (أنشفي). Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It was revealed in the language of India (الهند), meaning "Drink (اشربي)." And in one narration: Abyssinian (حبشيّة). وَيا سَماءُ أَقْلِعِي (11:44) (and O sky, withhold!') meaning: Stop (أمسكي). The calling of the earth and the sky with that by which rational beings (العقلاء) are called indicates the perfection of power (القدرة) and might (الاقتدار), and that these immense bodies are subservient to His creation (تكوينه) in them of whatever He wills, not refusing Him, knowing His majesty and greatness, and immediately complying with His command without delay. وَغِيضَ الْماءُ (11:44) (and the water subsided,) meaning: and decreased. وَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ (11:44) (and the decree was fulfilled,) meaning: and what He promised regarding the destruction of the disbelievers and the salvation of the believers was accomplished. وَاسْتَوَتْ عَلَى الْجُودِيِ (11:44) (and it rested on the Jūdī.) meaning: and it settled upon it, and it is a mountain in Mosul (الموصِل). وَقِيلَ بُعْداً لِلْقَوْمِ الظَّالِمِينَ (11:44) (And it was said, 'Away with the wrongdoing lot!') Its origin is (بَعُدَ بُعْداً بعيداً), meaning a distance from which return is not hoped for. Then it was borrowed for destruction (للهلاك) and specified for the invocation of evil (دعاءِ السّوءِ). It was said: The verse is of the utmost eloquence (الفصاحة) due to the grandeur of its wording (لفظها), the excellence of its composition (نظمها), and its indication of the essence of the situation, coupled with conciseness (الإيجاز) free from deficiency (الإخلال). The presentation of the verbs in the passive voice (البناء للمفعول) indicates the magnification of the Agent (الفاعل), and that He is self-evident (متعيّن) and needs no mention, since the imagination does not turn to anyone else, due to the knowledge that no one can perform such actions except the One, the All-dominant (الواحد القهّار).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) in a hadith: The Ark circled, and the waves struck it until it reached Mecca (مكّة) and circumambulated the House (البيت). The entire world drowned except for the location of the House. The House was only named al-Atiq (العتيق) because it was freed (أعتق) from drowning. The water continued to pour down from the sky for forty mornings, and springs (العيون) [gushed] from the earth, until the Ark rose and touched the sky. He (peace be upon him) said: Noah (peace be upon him) raised His hand and said: "O Rahman, perfect (اتقن)." Its interpretation is: "O Lord, make it good (أحسن)." Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded the earth to swallow its water, and that is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: يا أَرْضُ ابْلَعِي ماءَكِ وَيا سَماءُ أَقْلِعِي (11:44) meaning: Stop (أمسكي). وَغِيضَ الْماءُ وَقُضِيَ الْأَمْرُ وَاسْتَوَتْ عَلَى الْجُودِيِ (11:44) So the earth swallowed its water. The water of the sky intended to enter the earth, but the earth refused to accept it and said: "Allah only commanded me to swallow my water." So the water of the sky remained on the surface of the earth, and the Ark settled upon Mount al-Judi (الجوديّ), which is a great mountain in Mosul (الموصل). Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, sent Gabriel (جبرئيل), who drove the water to the seas around the world.

And al-Ayyashi narrated something close to some of what this hadith contained, which is the supplication of Noah (peace be upon him) and the story of the earth's refusal.

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to Noah (peace be upon him) while He was in the Ark that He should circumambulate the House (البيت) for a week. So He circumambulated the House as He had revealed to Him. Then He descended into the water up to His knees and extracted a chest (تابوتاً) containing the bones of Adam (آدم). He carried it inside the Ark until He had circumambulated as much as Allah willed Him to circumambulate. Then He arrived at the gate of Kufa (الكوفة), in the middle of its mosque (مسجدها). It was there that Allah said to the earth: ابْلَعِي ماءَكِ (11:44) so it swallowed its water from the Mosque of Kufa (مسجد الكوفة), just as the water had begun from it. And the company (الجمع) that was with Noah (peace be upon him) in the Ark dispersed.

In al-Kafi, narrated from Imam al-Kazim (peace be upon him): Noah was in the Ark, and He remained in it as long as Allah willed. The Ark was commanded [to move], so He circumambulated the House, and that is the circumambulation for women (طواف للنساء). Noah (peace be upon him) then released it. Then Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to the mountains: "I am placing the Ark of My servant Noah (peace be upon him) upon one of you." So they stretched high and became lofty, but الْجُودِيِ (11:44)—which is a mountain near you—humbled itself. The Ark struck the mountain with its breast (بجؤجؤها). He said: Noah then said: "O Mari, perfect (اتقن)," and this is Syriac (بالسّريانية) for: "Lord, set right (ربّ اصلح)."

In al-Majma and al-Ayyashi: [A narration] close to it. He said: And it is a mountain in Mosul (الموصل).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Noah (peace be upon him) heard the creaking (صرير) of the Ark upon al-Judi (الْجُودِيِ), so He feared for it. He put His head out of a small window (كوّة) that was in it, raised His hand, and pointed with His finger, saying: "O Rahman, perfect (اتقن)." Its interpretation is: "Lord, make it good (ربّ أحسن)."

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He was asked how long Noah (peace be upon him) and those with Him remained in the Ark until the water subsided and they exited it. He replied: They remained in it for seven days and nights. He circumambulated the House for a week, then اسْتَوَتْ عَلَى الْجُودِيِ (11:44) which is the Euphrates of Kufa (فرات الكوفة). And in one narration: And He performed the ritual running (سعت) between al-Safa (الصّفا) and al-Marwah (المروة).

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The water rose fifteen cubits (ذراعاً) above every mountain and every plain.

I say: Perhaps this amount of rise was after it had covered everything, and every plain and mountain was hidden within it.

In al-Khisal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): When the days of the Flood (الطّوفان) came, Noah (peace be upon him) called upon the waters of the earth, and they responded to Him, except for the bitter water (الماء المرّ) and the sulfur water (الكبريت).

(٤٥) وَنادى نُوحٌ رَبَّهُ فَقالَ رَبِّ إِنَّ ابْنِي مِنْ أَهْلِي وَإِنَّ وَعْدَكَ الْحَقُ (And Noah called out to his Lord, and said, 'My Lord, my son is indeed one of my family, and Your promise is surely true) And You had promised to save my family. وَأَنْتَ أَحْكَمُ الْحاكِمِينَ (and You are the wisest of all judges.') meaning, the most just (أعدلهم) and the most knowledgeable (وأعلمهم) among them. (٤٦) قالَ يا نُوحُ إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ مِنْ أَهْلِكَ (He said, 'O Noah, he is not of your family) meaning, those whom We promised you salvation for, because he is not upon your religion (دينك).

In al-Majma', al-Ayyashi, and al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah said to Noah: إِنَّهُ لَيْسَ مِنْ أَهْلِكَ (he is not of your family) because he was opposed to Him, and He made those who followed him to be of his family (أهلِهِ). إِنَّهُ عَمَلٌ غَيْرُ صالِحٍ (Indeed, he is an unrighteous deed.) This is a justification (تعليل) for negating his being of his family. His very essence (ذاته) was made an unrighteous deed (عملاً غير صالح) as an exaggeration in his condemnation (ذمّة). And it was recited (قرئ) as عَمِلَ (in the past tense form) and غَيْرَ with a fathah (بالفتح), meaning: he performed an unrighteous deed (عملاً غير صالح).

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): "How do they recite this verse?" It was said: "Some people recite إِنَّهُ عَمَلٌ غَيْرُ صالِحٍ (Indeed, he is an unrighteous deed), and some of them recite إِنَّهُ عَمِلَ غَيْرَ صالِحٍ (Indeed, he performed an unrighteous deed)." So, regarding the one who recites إِنَّهُ عَمَلٌ غَيْرُ صالِحٍ (Indeed, he is an unrighteous deed), He (peace be upon him) negated him from his father, saying: "No, he was indeed his son, but when he disobeyed Allah, He negated him from his father. Likewise, whoever among us does not obey Allah is not one of us." And in another narration, He (peace be upon him) negated him from him when he opposed him in his religion (دينِه).

And al-Ayyashi narrated that which is in the meaning of the second narration. فَلا تَسْئَلْنِ ما لَيْسَ لَكَ بِهِ عِلْمٌ (So do not ask Me about that of which you have no knowledge.) meaning, that which you do not know whether it is correct or not, until you know its essence (كنهه). And it was recited (قريء) as تَسْأَلَنَّ with a fathah on the lām (بفتح الّلام), and a shaddah (تشديد) on the nūn with a fathah (المفتوحة), and with a kasrah (وبكسر) on the nūn with a shaddah (المشدّدة), and with the affirmation of the yā' (واثبات الياءِ). إِنِّي أَعِظُكَ أَنْ تَكُونَ مِنَ الْجاهِلِينَ. (I advise you lest you should be among the ignorant.')

(٤٧) قالَ رَبِّ إِنِّي أَعُوذُ بِكَ أَنْ أَسْئَلَكَ ** regarding what is to come (in the future) ما لَيْسَ لِي بِهِ عِلْمٌ ** that whose correctness I have no knowledge of, out of respect for Your discipline (أدبك) and taking heed of Your admonition (موعظتك). وَإِلَّا تَغْفِرْ لِي ** what has occurred from me of the asking (the previous request) وَتَرْحَمْنِي ** through repentance (التوبة) and favor upon me (التفضل عليّ) أَكُنْ مِنَ الْخاسِرِينَ ** in deeds (أعمالاً). (47) He said, 'My Lord! I seek Your refuge from asking You what I have no knowledge of. And unless You forgive me and have mercy upon me, I shall be among the losers.' He said this by way of submission (الخضوع) to Allah, humility (التذلّل), and lowliness (الاستكانة).

(٤٨) قِيلَ يا نُوحُ اهْبِطْ بِسَلامٍ مِنَّا ** (48) (It was said,) 'O Noah, disembark with peace from Us, Descend from the Ark, safe from adversities, protected from Our direction. وَبَرَكاتٍ عَلَيْكَ ** and blessings upon you, And blessed upon you. And the blessings (البركات) are the growing good things (الخيرات النّامية). وَعَلى أُمَمٍ مِمَّنْ مَعَكَ ** and upon nations from those who are with you.' Meaning, those in the Ark, because they were groups (جماعات), or because the nations (الأمم) would branch out from them. وَأُمَمٌ سَنُمَتِّعُهُمْ ** And nations whom We shall let enjoy [life], That is, and from those who are with you, أُمَمٌ سَنُمَتِّعُهُمْ ** nations whom We shall let enjoy [life], in the world (الدنيا). ثُمَّ يَمَسُّهُمْ مِنَّا عَذابٌ أَلِيمٌ ** then a painful punishment from Us shall befall them.' He meant by them the disbelievers (الكفّار) from the progeny of those who were with him.

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): So Noah descended at al-Mawsil from the Ark, along with the eighty, and they built the City of the Eighty (مدينة الثمانين). And Noah (peace be upon him) had a daughter who embarked with Him in the Ark, and mankind propagated from her. And from this is the saying of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم): "Noah is one of the two fathers."

(٤٩) تِلْكَ مِنْ أَنْباءِ الْغَيْبِ نُوحِيها إِلَيْكَ ما كُنْتَ تَعْلَمُها أَنْتَ وَلا قَوْمُكَ مِنْ قَبْلِ هذا فَاصْبِرْ إِنَّ الْعاقِبَةَ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ (٤٩) (49) These are accounts of the Unseen which We reveal to you. Neither you nor your people knew them before this. So be patient; indeed, the outcome is for the Godwary.

تِلْكَ ** refers to the story of Noah (peace be upon him). مِنْ أَنْباءِ الْغَيْبِ ** meaning, some of them. نُوحِيها إِلَيْكَ ما كُنْتَ تَعْلَمُها أَنْتَ وَلا قَوْمُكَ مِنْ قَبْلِ هذا فَاصْبِرْ ** [So be patient] regarding the hardships of the Message (الرسالة) and the harm inflicted by the people, just as Noah (peace be upon him) was patient. إِنَّ الْعاقِبَةَ ** The outcome, in this world (الدنيا) through victory (الظّفر) and in the Hereafter (الآخِرة) through triumph (الفوز). لِلْمُتَّقِينَ ** [Is for those who are] Godwary, [abstaining] from polytheism (الشرك) and acts of disobedience (المعاصي).

Al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): Noah (peace be upon him) remained among his people for three hundred years, calling them to Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, but they did not respond to Him. He intended to supplicate against them, but at sunrise, twelve thousand tribes of the angels of the lowest heaven (السماء الدّنيا) met him—and they are the great ones among the angels. Noah (peace be upon him) said to them: "Who are you?" They replied: "We are twelve thousand tribes of the angels of the lowest heaven. The thickness of the lowest heaven is a journey of five hundred years, and [the distance] from the lowest heaven to the earth (الدنيا) is five hundred years. We departed at the break of dawn and met you at this time, and we ask you not to supplicate against your people." Noah said: "I have granted them a respite of three hundred years." When six hundred years had passed over them and they had not believed, he intended to supplicate against them. Then twelve thousand tribes of the angels of the second heaven (السماءِ الثانية) met him. Noah (peace be upon him) said: "Who are you?" They replied: "We are twelve thousand tribes of the angels of the second heaven. The thickness of the second heaven is a journey of five hundred years, and [the distance] from the second heaven to the lowest heaven (السماء الدنيا) is a journey of five hundred years, and the thickness of the lowest heaven is a journey of five hundred years, and [the distance] from the lowest heaven to the earth (الدنيا) is a journey of five hundred years. We departed at sunrise and met you at mid-morning (ضحوة). We ask you not to supplicate against your people." Noah (peace be upon him) said: "I have granted them a respite of three hundred years." When nine hundred years had passed over them and they had not believed, he intended to supplicate against them. Then the Mighty and Majestic revealed: أَنَّهُ لَنْ يُؤْمِنَ مِنْ قَوْمِكَ إِلَّا مَنْ قَدْ آمَنَ فَلا تَبْتَئِسْ بِما كانُوا يَفْعَلُونَ وَقالَ نُوحٌ رَبِّ لا تَذَرْ عَلَى الْأَرْضِ مِنَ الْكافِرِينَ دَيَّاراً إِنَّكَ إِنْ تَذَرْهُمْ يُضِلُّوا عِبادَكَ وَلا يَلِدُوا إِلَّا فاجِراً كَفَّاراً (that none of your people will believe except those who have already believed, so do not grieve for what they have been doing. And Noah said, 'My Lord! Do not leave any inhabitant of the faithless on the earth. For if You leave them, they will lead Your servants astray, and will not be begot anyone but vicious ingrates.') So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, commanded him to plant date palms (النخل). He proceeded to plant the date palms, and his people would pass by him, mocking him and ridiculing him, saying: "An old man who has reached nine hundred years is planting date palms!" And they used to pelt him with stones. When fifty years had passed for that, and the date palms had grown and become strong, He commanded him to cut them down. So they mocked him and said: "The date palms have reached their full growth!" This is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: وَكُلَّما مَرَّ عَلَيْهِ مَلَأٌ مِنْ قَوْمِهِ سَخِرُوا مِنْهُ قالَ إِنْ تَسْخَرُوا مِنَّا فَإِنَّا نَسْخَرُ مِنْكُمْ كَما تَسْخَرُونَ فَسَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ (And whenever a council of his people passed by him, they derided him. He said, 'If you deride us, we will deride you just as you deride [us].
