Soon you will know') Then Allah commanded him to build the Ark (السفينة) and commanded Gabriel (جبرئيل) to descend upon him and teach him how to build it. He estimated its length on the ground to be one thousand two hundred cubits (ذراع), its width eight hundred cubits, and its height in the sky eighty cubits. He said: "O Lord, who will help me build it?" So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to him: "Call out among your people that whoever helps Me with it and carves anything from it, what he carves will turn into gold and silver." So Noah (peace be upon him) called out to them with that, and they helped him with it, yet they continued to mock him, saying: "A ship being built on dry land!"

In al-Ikmal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): As for the delay [in the punishment] for Noah (peace be upon him), when he sought the descent of the punishment upon his people from the heaven, Allah the Almighty sent Gabriel (جبرئيل), the Trustworthy Spirit (الرّوح الأمين), with seven date stones (نوايات). He said: "O Prophet of Allah, Allah the Almighty says to you: 'These are My creations and My servants. I will not destroy them with a thunderbolt from My thunderbolts except after confirming the call (الدّعوة) and establishing the conclusive proof (الحجّة). So, resume your diligence (اجتهادَك) in calling your people, for I shall reward you for it. And plant these date stones (النّوى), for in their sprouting, growing, and ripening when they bear fruit, there will be relief (الفرَجَ) and deliverance (الخلاص) for you. So, give glad tidings of this to the believers who followed you.'" When the trees sprouted, strengthened their roots (تأزّرت), grew trunks (تسوّقت), branched out (اغتصنت), and the dates ripened (زهى التمر) after a long time, he requested Allah to fulfill the promise (العِدّة). So Allah the Almighty commanded him to plant the stones (نوى) of those trees and to resume patience and diligence, and to confirm the proof upon his people. He informed the groups (الطوائف) who had believed in him of this. Three hundred men among them apostatized (ارتدّ), saying: "If what Noah (peace be upon him) claims were true, there would be no failure in the promise of his Lord." Then Allah the Almighty continued to command him, each time, to plant them again and again, until he had planted them seven times. Those groups of believers continued to apostatize, group after group, until [the remaining number] returned to seventy-odd men (نيّف وسبعين رجلاً). At that point, Allah revealed to him and said: "O Noah, now the morning has broken from the night, so that We may help you when the truth has declared its essence (محضه) and become pure from impurity (الكدر) through the apostasy of everyone whose nature (طينته) was wicked (خبيثة). For had I destroyed the disbelievers and left those who apostatized from the groups that had believed in you, My previous promise to the believers among your people who purified their monotheism (التوحيد) and held fast to the rope of your prophethood—that I would grant them succession (استخلفهم) on earth, establish their religion for them, and replace their fear with security (الأمن)—would not have been fulfilled, so that worship (العبادة) might be purely for Me with the disappearance of polytheism (الشرك) from their hearts. And how could succession (الاستخلاف), establishment (التّمكين), and the granting of security from Me be possible for them, given what I knew of the weakness of conviction of those who apostatized, the wickedness of their nature (طينتهِم), and the evil of their inner thoughts (سرائرهم) which were the results of hypocrisy (النفاق) and the deep-rootedness of misguidance (الضلالة)? For had they tasted the sovereignty (الملك) granted to the believers at the time of succession, when their enemies were destroyed, they would have inhaled the scents of its attributes, the bitterness of their hypocrisy would have solidified, the corruption of the misguidance in their hearts would have erupted, and they would have openly shown enmity to their brethren and fought them seeking leadership (الرّياسة) and exclusivity in command and prohibition (الأمر والنهي). And how could there be establishment in religion and the spread of the matter among the believers with the stirring up of tribulations (الفتن) and the waging of wars? Never! So, اصْنَعِ الْفُلْكَ بِأَعْيُنِنا وَوَحْيِنا (build the ark under Our observation and by Our revelation)."

In al-Uyun, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): It was said to him: "O son of the Messenger of Allah, for what reason did Allah drown the entire world in the time of Noah, when among them were children and those who had no sin?" He replied: "There were no children among them, because Allah made the loins of Noah's people and the wombs of their women barren (أعقم) for forty years, so their progeny ceased. Thus, they drowned, and there was no child among them. Allah would not destroy with His punishment one who has no sin. As for the rest of Noah's people, they were drowned due to their denial of Allah's Prophet Noah, and the others were drowned due to their contentment (رضاهم) with the denial of the deniers. And whoever is absent from an affair but is content with it is like one who witnessed it."

In al-Kafi and al-Ikmal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): When the water uncovered (حسر) (1) the bones of the dead, and Noah (peace be upon him) saw that, he became extremely distressed (جزعاً شديداً) and grieved over it. So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed: "This is your doing; you supplicated against them." He said: "O Lord, I seek Your forgiveness and repent to You." So Allah revealed to him: "Eat black grapes (العنب الأسود) to remove your grief."

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): The lifespans of Noah's people were three hundred years.

In al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Noah lived two thousand three hundred years. Of that, eight hundred and fifty years were before he was commissioned [as a prophet], one thousand years minus fifty years (950 years) were spent among his people calling them, and five hundred years were after he descended from the Ark (السفينة) and the water subsided (نضب الماء). He founded cities (مصّر الأمصار) and settled his children in the lands. Then the Angel of Death (ملك الموت) came to him while he was in the sun. He said: "Peace be upon you (السلام عليك)." Noah (peace be upon him) returned the greeting and said: "What brings you, O Angel of Death?" He replied: "I have come to seize your soul." He said: "Allow me to move from the sun into the shade." He said to him: "Yes." So he moved, and then he said: "O Angel of Death, all that has passed me of this world (الدنيا) is like my moving from the sun to the shade. So proceed with what you have been commanded." Then he seized his soul.

And narrated from him (peace be upon him): Noah (peace be upon him) lived five hundred years after the Flood (الطّوفان). Then Gabriel (جبرئيل) came to him and said: "O Noah, your turn (نوبتك) has ended and your days are complete. Look at the Greatest Name (الاسم الأكبر), the inheritance of knowledge (ميراث العلم), and the signs of the knowledge of prophethood (آثار علم النبوّة) that are with you, and hand them over to your son Sam. For I do not leave the earth unless there is a scholar (عالم) upon it through whom My obedience is known, and through whom My guidance (هداي) is known, and salvation (النجاة) exists between the passing of one Prophet (مقبض النّبيّ) and the commissioning of the next Prophet (مبعث النّبيّ الآخر). I would not leave people without a proof (حجّة) for Me, a caller to Me, a guide to My path (سبيلي), and one who knows My command. For I have decreed that I shall appoint a guide (هادياً) for every people, through whom I guide the fortunate (السعداءَ) and who serves as a proof for Me against the wretched (الأشقياءِ)." He said: So Noah handed over the Greatest Name (الاسم الأكبر), the inheritance of knowledge, and the signs of the knowledge of prophethood to Sam. As for Ham and Japheth (يافث), they did not possess knowledge that would benefit them. He said: And Noah gave them the glad tidings of Hud (يهود) and commanded them to follow him, and commanded them to open the testament (الوَصيّة) every year and look into it, and that it should be a festival (عيداً) for them.

(1) حسَرَه يحسره means he uncovered it, and the thing حسوراً means it was uncovered.

(٥٠) وَإِلى عادٍ أَخاهُمْ هُوداً أَخاهُمْ ** (50) And to [the people of] 'Ad [We sent] their brother Hud. meaning one of them, as its explanation preceded in Surat al-A'raf. قالَ يا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ وحده ما لَكُمْ مِنْ إِلهٍ غَيْرُهُ ** He said: O my people, worship Allah alone. You have no god other than Him. وقرء بالجرِّ And it was read with al-jarr (the genitive case). **إِنْ أَنْتُمْ إِلَّا مُفْتَرُونَ ** You are but fabricators. against Allah by taking idols as partners and making them intercessors.

(٥١) يا قَوْمِ لا أَسْئَلُكُمْ عَلَيْهِ أَجْراً إِنْ أَجْرِيَ إِلَّا عَلَى الَّذِي فَطَرَنِي ** (51) O my people! I do not ask you any reward for it. My reward lies only with Him who originated me. Every messenger addressed his people with this, in order to remove the accusation and to purify the sincerity of the advice, for it will not be effective as long as it is mixed with covetousness (المطامع). أَفَلا تَعْقِلُونَ ** Do you not use your intellects so that you may know the truthful one (المحقّ) from the false one (المبطل), and the correct (الصّواب) from the error (الخَطَأ).

(٥٢) وَيا قَوْمِ اسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّكُمْ ثُمَّ تُوبُوا إِلَيْهِ ** (52) O my people! Plead with your Lord for forgiveness, then turn to Him penitently. Seek the forgiveness of Allah through faith, then seek access to it through repentance (التّوبة). يُرْسِلِ السَّماءَ عَلَيْكُمْ مِدْراراً ** He will send down for you abundant rain from the sky, Abundant in pouring (الدّرّ). وَيَزِدْكُمْ قُوَّةً إِلى قُوَّتِكُمْ ** and increase your strength with [more] strength. And He will multiply your strength (قوّتكم). It was said: He encouraged them toward faith by [promising] abundant rain and increased strength, because they were owners of crops (زروع) and gardens (بساتين), and they boasted (يدلّون) of strength (القوّة) and might (البطش). وَلا تَتَوَلَّوْا ** Do not turn away, And do not turn away (تعرضوا) from me and from what I call you to, مُجْرِمِينَ persisting (مصرّين) in your criminality (اجرامكم).

(٥٣) قالُوا يا هُودُ ما جِئْتَنا بِبَيِّنَةٍ ** (53) They said, 'O Hud, you have not brought us any manifest proof, with a proof (حجّة) that indicates the correctness of your claim (دعواك). This (statement of theirs) is a lie and a denial (جحود) due to the extremity of their obstinacy (عنادهم) and their disregard (عدم اعتدادهم) for the miracles (المعجزاتِ) that came to them. وَما نَحْنُ بِتارِكِي آلِهَتِنا ** (and we are not abandoners of our gods) (meaning) we are not abandoners of their worship (عبادتهم). عَنْ قَوْلِكَ وَما نَحْنُ لَكَ بِمُؤْمِنِينَ ** (because of your saying, and we are not believers in you.) (This is) to make him despair (اقناط) of response (الإجابة) and affirmation (التصديق).

(٥٤) إِنْ نَقُولُ إِلَّا اعْتَراكَ ** (it afflicted you) بَعْضُ آلِهَتِنا بِسُوءٍ ** with madness (بجنون) because of your reviling them and turning people away from them, and thus you speak the speech of the insane. إِنْ نَقُولُ إِلَّا اعْتَراكَ بَعْضُ آلِهَتِنا بِسُوءٍ ۗ قالَ إِنِّي أُشْهِدُ اللهَ وَاشْهَدُوا أَنِّي بَرِيءٌ مِمَّا تُشْرِكُونَ (54) (We only say that some of our gods have afflicted you with evil." He said, "Indeed, I call Allah to witness, and you too bear witness, that I am absolved of what you associate [with Him]) (54)

(55) مِنْ دُونِهِ ** (besides Him) [It is] from your associating deities besides Him. فَكِيدُونِي جَمِيعاً ثُمَّ لا تُنْظِرُونِ ** (So scheme against me, all of you, and do not respite me) He confronted them with this statement despite their strength, their might, their large number, and their thirst for shedding his blood, [doing so] trusting in Allah and relying upon His protection of him, and belittling them and their plotting, even if they gathered against him and conspired for his destruction.

(٥٦) إِنِّي تَوَكَّلْتُ عَلَى اللهِ رَبِّي وَرَبِّكُمْ ** (56) I have put my trust in Allah, my Lord and your Lord. This is an affirmation for Him (Hūd عليه السلام). The meaning is: Even if you exert the utmost of your effort, you will not harm me, for I am reliant upon Allah, trusting in His word. He is my Master and your Master. Nothing that He does not will shall befall me, and you are not capable of anything He has not decreed. مَا مِنْ دَابَّةٍ إِلَّا هُوَ آخِذٌ بِناصِيَتِها ** there is no creature but He holds it by its forelock. Meaning: There is no creature except that He is its Master, dominant over it, directing it according to what He wills for it. And the holding by the forelock (الْأَخْذُ بِالنَّاصِيَةِ) is a representation of that. **إِنَّ رَبِّي عَلى صِراطٍ مُسْتَقِيمٍ ** Indeed my Lord is on a straight path. He is upon truth and justice. No one who seeks refuge with Him shall be lost, and no wrongdoer shall escape Him.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (prayers of Allah be upon him): It means that He is upon truth, rewarding good deeds with good and evil deeds with evil, and He pardons whom He wills and forgives, glory be to Him and exalted is He.

(٥٧) فَإِنْ تَوَلَّوْا (57) But if you turn away, If you turn away, فَقَدْ أَبْلَغْتُكُمْ ما أُرْسِلْتُ بِهِ إِلَيْكُمْ ** then I have communicated to you what I was sent to you with. then I have fulfilled what was incumbent upon me regarding the communication and the establishment of the proof (الحجّة). وَيَسْتَخْلِفُ رَبِّي قَوْماً غَيْرَكُمْ My Lord will replace you with another people, This is a threat (وعيد) to them of destruction (الإهلاك) and replacement (الاستبدال). وَلا تَضُرُّونَهُ شَيْئاً ** and you will not harm Him in the least. by your turning away. إِنَّ رَبِّي عَلى كُلِّ شَيْءٍ حَفِيظٌ ** Indeed, my Lord is the Preserver of all things." He is a Watcher (رقيبٌ), so your deeds are not hidden from Him, nor is He heedless of holding you accountable (مؤاخذتكم).

(٥٨) **وَلَمَّا جاءَ أَمْرُنا نَجَّيْنا هُوداً وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِنَّا وَنَجَّيْناهُمْ مِنْ عَذابٍ غَلِيظٍ ** (58) So when Our command came, We delivered Hūd and those who had believed with him by a mercy from Us, and We delivered them from a severe punishment.

This is a repetition to explain what He saved them from, or the intended meaning by it is their deliverance from the punishment of the Hereafter as well, and the implication that the destroyed ones, just as they were punished in this world (بالدنيا), they will be punished in the Hereafter with the severe punishment (بالعذاب الغليظ).

(59) وَتِلْكَ عادٌ جَحَدُوا بِآياتِ رَبِّهِمْ ** (59) And those were [the people of] 'Ad. They denied the signs of their Lord, They disbelieved in them. وَعَصَوْا رُسُلَهُ ** (and disobeyed His apostles,) Because if they disobeyed their messenger, they have disobeyed all the messengers of Allah. وَاتَّبَعُوا أَمْرَ كُلِّ جَبَّارٍ عَنِيدٍ (and followed the command of every obstinate tyrant.) Meaning: their leaders, the callers (الدّعاة) to the denial (تكذيب) of the messengers (الرّسل).

(60) وَأُتْبِعُوا فِي هذِهِ الدُّنْيا لَعْنَةً وَيَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ (60) And they were pursued by a curse in this world and on the Day of Resurrection. meaning, the curse was made to follow them in both abodes (الدارين), casting them down (تكبهم) (1) into the punishment. أَلا إِنَّ عاداً كَفَرُوا رَبَّهُمْ أَلا بُعْداً لِعادٍ قَوْمِ هُودٍ (60) Look! Indeed, ‘Ad defied their Lord. Look! Away with ‘Ad, the people of Hud! This is a supplication against them for destruction (بالهلاك) and an indication that they were deserving of what descended upon them. In the repetition of أَلا and the re-mentioning of ‘Ad is a magnification of their affair (تفظيع لأمرهم), urging people to take heed (الإعتبار) from their condition and beware of actions like theirs. And they were only called قَوْمِ هُودٍ (the people of Hud) to distinguish them from ‘Ad of Iram (عاد إرِمَ).

Al-Qummiyy (القمّيّ): Indeed, ‘Ad’s lands were in the desert (البادية) from the East to al-Ajfar (الأجفر) (1), a distance of four stages (أربعة منازل). They had much cultivation (زرع) and many date palms. They had long lifespans (أعمار طويلة) and tall bodies. Then they worshipped idols (الأصنام). Allah sent Hud to them, inviting them to Islam and to renounce the rivals (الأنداد). But they refused, did not believe in Hud, and harmed him. So, the sky withheld rain from them for seven years until they suffered drought (قحطوا).

Hud was a farmer (زرّاعاً) and used to irrigate the crops (الزّرع). A group of people came to his door seeking him. A gray-haired (شمطاء) (2), one-eyed (عوراء) (3) woman came out to them and asked, "Who are you?" They replied, "We are from such-and-such lands. Our lands have become barren (أجدبت), so we have come to Hud, asking him to supplicate to Allah so that it may rain and our lands may become fertile (يخصب)." She said, "If Hud’s prayer were answered, He would have prayed for Himself, for His crops have burned due to lack of water." They asked, "Where is He?" She said, "He is in such-and-such a place." So they came to him and said, "O Prophet of Allah, our lands have become barren and it has not rained. Ask Allah to make our lands fertile and send rain." So he prepared for the prayer (الصّلوة), prayed, and supplicated for them. Then he said to them, "Return, for you have been rained upon and your lands have become fertile." They said, "O Prophet of Allah, we saw something wondrous (عجباً)." He asked, "What did you see?" They replied, "We saw a gray-haired, one-eyed woman in your dwelling. She asked us, 'Who are you and whom do you seek?' We said, 'We came to Hud so that he may supplicate to Allah for us to receive rain.' She said, 'If Hud were a supplicator, he would have prayed for himself, for his crops have burned.'" Hud said, "That is my family (أهلي), and I supplicate to Allah for her long life (بطول البقاء)." They asked, "And how is that?" He said, "Because Allah has not created a believer except that he has an enemy who harms him, and she is my enemy. So, for my enemy to be someone whom I possess (أملكه) is better than for my enemy to be someone who possesses me (يملكني)."

So Hud remained among his people, inviting them to Allah and forbidding them from worshipping idols, until their lands became fertile and Allah sent down rain upon them. This is His saying, the Mighty and Majestic: يا قَوْمِ اسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّكُمْ (O my people, seek forgiveness from your Lord) and the subsequent verses (الآيات). When they did not believe, Allah sent upon them the al-Rih al-Sarsar (الريح الصرصر) (4), meaning the cold wind. This is the Almighty's saying in Surat al-Qamar: كَذَّبَتْ عادٌ فَكَيْفَ كانَ عَذابِي وَنُذُرِ إِنَّا أَرْسَلْنا عَلَيْهِمْ رِيحاً صَرْصَراً فِي يَوْمِ نَحْسٍ (18) ‘Ad denied [the truth]. So how was My punishment and warnings? (19) We sent against them a freezing wind on a day of persistent ill-omen, مُسْتَمِرٍّ (20) which plucked out mankind as if they were trunks of uprooted palm trees. And it is narrated in Surat al-Haqqah where He said: وَأَمَّا عادٌ فَأُهْلِكُوا بِرِيحٍ صَرْصَرٍ عاتِيَةٍ سَخَّرَها عَلَيْهِمْ سَبْعَ لَيالٍ وَثَمانِيَةَ أَيَّامٍ حُسُوماً (6) But as for ‘Ad, they were destroyed by a howling, fierce wind, (7) which He imposed upon them for seven nights and eight days continuously. He said: The moon was afflicted (منحوساً) by a clamor (بزجل) for seven nights and eight days.

I say: The complete explanation of their eradication (استيصالهم) has already preceded in Surat al-A‘raf.

(1) The Almighty's saying فَمَنْ يَمْشِي مُكِبًّا (Is he who walks prone upon his face) [Q. 67:22]—meaning, thrown down upon his face. This is said of every traveler (ساير), meaning walker, whether on four legs or not. It is said: Kababtu fulānan kabban (كببت فلاناً كبّاً), meaning I threw him down upon his face, and fa-akabba huwa (فاكبّ هو) with the alif (الألف). This is one of the rare instances where the triliteral verb (ثلاثيّها) is transitive (يعدّى) but its quadriliteral form (رباعيّها) is not. (M.)

(1) Al-Ajfar (الأجفر) is a place between al-Khuraymiyyah (الخريميّة) and Fayd (فيد) (Q.). What is meant is their lands were on the eastern side of al-Ajfar, at a distance of four stages from it.

(2) In the Hadith: There is no harm in cutting (جزّ) or plucking (نتفه) gray hair (الشمط), and cutting it is more beloved to me than plucking it. Al-Shamat (الشمط), with vocalization (بالتّحريك), is the whiteness of the hair of the head mixed with its blackness. The man is ashmat (اشمط) and the woman is shamṭā’ (شمطاء).

(3) The eye became ‘awirat (عورت) (one-eyed) with ‘awaran (عوراً) (from the pattern of ta‘iba (تعب))—it became defective or sunken. The man is a‘war (اعور) and the female is ‘awrā’ (عوراء). (M.)

(4) Al-Sirrah (الصِّرة) with a kasrah (بالكسر) is the severity of cold (شدّة البرد) or coldness, like al-Sarr (الصرّ) in both meanings, and the loudest shouting (أشدّ الصياح). And with a fathah (بالفتح), it is severity (الشدّة) from distress (الكرب), war (الحرب), and heat (الحرّ). A wind that is ṣarr (صرّ) or ṣarṣar (صرصر) is intensely loud or cold. And ṣurra al-nabāt (صُرّ النّبات) with a ḍammah (بالضّم) means the coldness (الصرّ) afflicted the plant.

(٦١) وَإِلى ثَمُودَ أَخاهُمْ صالِحاً قالَ يا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ ما لَكُمْ مِنْ إِلهٍ غَيْرُهُ هُوَ أَنْشَأَكُمْ مِنَ الْأَرْضِ ** (61) And to Thamūd [We sent] their brother Ṣāliḥ. He said, 'O my people, worship Allah! You have no god other than Him. He brought you forth from the earth He formed you from it and nothing else, for He created Adam and the materials of the drops (النطف) from which He created his progeny, from the dust (التّراب). وَاسْتَعْمَرَكُمْ فِيها ** and settled you in it. He kept you alive for a lifespan (العمر), or He commanded you to cultivate it (عمارتها). فَاسْتَغْفِرُوهُ ثُمَّ تُوبُوا إِلَيْهِ إِنَّ رَبِّي قَرِيبٌ ** So ask forgiveness of Him, then turn to Him penitently. Indeed my Lord is near, to you, a Responder (مُجِيبٌ) to whoever calls upon Him.

٦٢ قالُوا يا صالِحُ قَدْ كُنْتَ فِينا مَرْجُوًّا قَبْلَ هذا ** (٦٢) They said, 'O Salih, you were a source of hope for us before this. We hoped for good from you because of the signs (مخايلة) that used to appear from you (١). So we used to seek guidance from you in our planning (تدابيرنا) and consult you in our affairs (أمورنا). But now our hope in you is cut off, and we know that there is no good in you. أَتَنْهانا أَنْ نَعْبُدَ ما يَعْبُدُ آباؤُنا وَإِنَّنا لَفِي شَكٍّ مِمَّا تَدْعُونا إِلَيْهِ ** Are you forbidding us to worship what our fathers used to worship? We are indeed in grave doubt concerning that to which you invite us, concerning monotheism (التّوحيد) and dissociation (التَّبري) from idols (الأوثان). مُرِيبٍ ** causing suspicion (الرّيبة) or possessing suspicion (ذي ريبة).

(٦٣) قالَ يا قَوْمِ أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُ عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّي ** manifest proof (بيان) and insight (بصيرة) وَآتانِي مِنْهُ رَحْمَةً ** prophethood (نبوة) فَمَنْ يَنْصُرُنِي مِنَ اللهِ ** So who will protect me from His punishment (عذابه) إِنْ عَصَيْتُهُ ** in conveying His message (رسالته) and forbidding association of partners (الإشراك) with Him فَما تَزِيدُونَنِي ** then, by your following me غَيْرَ تَخْسِيرٍ ** other than that I attribute you to loss (الخسران), or other than that you cause me loss (تُخَسِّرُونِي) by invalidating what Allah has granted me.

(63) He said, 'O my people! Tell me, if I stand on a manifest proof from my Lord, and He has given me a mercy from Him, who will help me against Allah if I disobey Him? So you will not add anything to me except loss.

(٦٤) وَيا قَوْمِ هذِهِ ناقَةُ اللهِ لَكُمْ آيَةً فَذَرُوها تَأْكُلْ فِي أَرْضِ اللهِ ** (64) O my people! This she-camel of Allah is a sign for you. Leave her to graze in Allah’s earth, —She grazes its vegetation and drinks its water— وَلا تَمَسُّوها بِسُوءٍ فَيَأْخُذَكُمْ عَذابٌ قَرِيبٌ ** and do not touch her with any harm, or a swift punishment will seize you. —swift (عاجلٌ). (٦٥) فَعَقَرُوها فَقالَ تَمَتَّعُوا فِي دارِكُمْ ** (65) But they hamstrung her. Then He said, 'Enjoy yourselves in your homes —live in your dwellings (منازلكم) or your land (بلدكم)— ثَلاثَةَ أَيَّامٍ ** for three days. —then you will perish— ذلِكَ وَعْدٌ غَيْرُ مَكْذُوبٍ. That is a promise not to be belied.' (٦٦) فَلَمَّا جاءَ أَمْرُنا نَجَّيْنا صالِحاً وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِنَّا وَمِنْ خِزْيِ يَوْمِئِذٍ ** (66) So when Our command came, We delivered Salih and those who had believed with him by a mercy from Us, and from the disgrace of that day. —meaning, We delivered them from the disgrace (خزي) of that day, its humiliation (ذلّه), and its scandal (فضيحه). And there is no disgrace greater than the disgrace of one whose destruction was by the wrath of Allah and His might. Or, by 'that day' (يومئذ), the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) is intended. And it was recited يومئذ with fathah on the mīm, based on its indeclinability (بنائه) when it is annexed to إذ— إِنَّ رَبَّكَ هُوَ الْقَوِيُّ الْعَزِيزُ ** Indeed your Lord is the All-strong, the All-mighty. —The One capable of everything and dominant over it.

footnote (١) And *المخايل* is the plural of *المخيلة*, which is that which occurs in the imagination (*الخيال*). By it, he means the signs (*الإمارات*). And *خلت الشّيء خيلاً ومخيلاً* means: I thought (*ظننته*) it.

(٦٧) وَأَخَذَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا الصَّيْحَةُ فَأَصْبَحُوا فِي دِيارِهِمْ جاثِمِينَ ** (67) And the Cry seized those who were wrongdoers, and they lay lifeless in their homes. [meaning] dead (ميّتين). The origin of al-juthūm (الجثوم) is remaining fixed in the place (اللّزوم في المكان). Its interpretation has already preceded in Surat al-A'raf (سورة الأعراف) along with the complete story. (٦٨) كَأَنْ لَمْ يَغْنَوْا فِيها ** (68) as if they had never lived there. [meaning] as if they had not resided therein while alive (أحياءً). *أَلا إِنَّ ثَمُودَ وقرئ منّوناً كَفَرُوا رَبَّهُمْ أَلا بُعْداً لِثَمُودَ. Behold, Thamud indeed defied their Lord. Behold, away with Thamud! (٦٩) وَلَقَدْ جاءَتْ رُسُلُنا إِبْراهِيمَ ** (69) Certainly Our messengers came to Abraham with the good news. [meaning] the angels (الملائكة). بِالْبُشْرى ** with the good news (بشارة) of a child. In al-Majma', narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They were four: Jibra'il (جبرئيل), Mika'il (ميكائيل), Israfil (إسرافيل), and Karubil (كروبيل) (1). And in it [(al-Majma')] and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Indeed, this good news was concerning Isma'il (اسمعيل) from Hajar (هاجر). And it will come in al-Ilal (العلل) and al-Ayyashi that it was concerning Ishaq (اسحق). قالُوا سَلاماً ** They said, 'Peace!' [meaning] We greet you with peace (سلّمنا عليك). سَلاماً ** meaning safety (سلامة). قالَ سَلامٌ ** He replied, 'Peace!' [meaning] your affair (أمركم) is peace (سلامٌ). And it was recited salim (سلم) with kasra (بالكسر) and sukūn (والسكون). فَما لَبِثَ أَنْ جاءَ بِعِجْلٍ حَنِيذٍ ** Soon he brought a roasted calf. roasted, well-cooked (مشوّي نضيج) (2). Al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): [meaning] ritually slaughtered (زكيّاً), roasted, well-cooked. And from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): [meaning] roasted, well-cooked. And from him (peace be upon him), he said: "Eat." So they said: "We will not eat until you inform us of its price (ثمنه)." He replied: "When you eat, say: بسم الله (In the Name of Allah), and when you finish, say: الحمد لله (All praise belongs to Allah)." He (the Imam) said: Then Jibra'il (جبرئيل) turned to his companions—and they were four, their leader being Jibra'il (جبرئيل)—and said: "It is rightful for Allah that He takes this one as a close friend (خليلاً*)."

(٧٠) فَلَمَّا رَأى أَيْدِيَهُمْ لا تَصِلُ إِلَيْهِ ** (70) When he saw that their hands did not reach for it, They were not extending their hands towards it. نَكِرَهُمْ ** He found them unfamiliar (أنكرهم). وَأَوْجَسَ مِنْهُمْ خِيفَةً ** he felt uneasy about them and conceived a fear of them. He concealed a fear (خوفاً) of them, lest they intended harm (مكروهاً) towards him. قالُوا لا تَخَفْ إِنَّا أُرْسِلْنا إِلى قَوْمِ لُوطٍ ** They said, 'Do not be afraid. We have been sent to the people of Lot.' We are angels sent to them with the punishment (بالعذاب). And We did not extend Our hands towards it only because We do not eat.

(٧١) وَامْرَأَتُهُ قائِمَةٌ ** (And his wife was standing by) She was hearing their conversation, and she is Sarah, the daughter of لا حج, and she is his maternal aunt's daughter. Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He only meant Sarah. فَضَحِكَتْ ** (and she laughed) out of joy, and she menstruated (حاضت) out of fright (الفزع). In al-Ilal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): It means she was astonished by their saying. And in al-Ma'ani, al-Majma', and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): She menstruated (حاضت). And al-Qummi (said): فَضَحِكَتْ ** (and she laughed), meaning she menstruated (حاضت), and her menstruation had ceased since a long time ago (دهر طويل). I say: And from this (meaning) is the saying of the poet: "And my covenant with Salma is that she is ضاحكاً in لبابة, and her breasts have not exceeded the حُق of being milked." And from this (meaning) is when the fruit [or the tree, variant reading خ ل] ضحكت when its sap (صمغها) flowed. فَبَشَّرْناها بِإِسْحاقَ وَمِنْ وَراءِ إِسْحاقَ يَعْقُوبَ ** (So We gave her the good news of [the birth of] Isaac, and of Jacob after Isaac.) meaning, and after him. And it was said that الوراء (after/behind) means the child of the child (grandchild). And it was recited يعقوب with the رفع (nominative case).

(٧٢) قالَتْ يا وَيْلَتى ** (72) She said, 'O woe is me! O wonder (يا عجبا). Its origin is in misfortune (الشر), but it is used for every terrible matter. أَأَلِدُ وَأَنَا عَجُوزٌ وَهذا بَعْلِي my spouse, شَيْخاً ** (an old man?) In al-Ilal, narrated from one of the two (Imams) (peace be upon them): And she on that day was ninety years old, and Abraham on that day was one hundred and twenty years old. إِنَّ هذا لَشَيْءٌ عَجِيبٌ ** Indeed, this is a wondrous thing.' Meaning the child from the two elderly people (الهرمين), and it is an expression of wonder according to custom (العادة), not according to (divine) power (القدرة).

(٧٣) قالُوا أَتَعْجَبِينَ مِنْ أَمْرِ اللهِ رَحْمَتُ اللهِ وَبَرَكاتُهُ عَلَيْكُمْ أَهْلَ الْبَيْتِ ** (73) They said, 'Are you astonished at the command of Allah? The mercy of Allah and His blessings are upon you, members of the household.' Meaning, this [blessing] and its likes are among the things with which Allah honors you, O people of the Household of Prophethood (أهل بيت النّبوة), so this is not a place for astonishment. إِنَّهُ حَمِيدٌ ** [He is] the Doer of that by which He deserves praise (الحمد), مَجِيدٌ ** Abundant in goodness (الخير) and benevolence (الإحسان).

Al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) that he said: Allah revealed to Abraham that a child would be born to him. So he told Sarah, and she said: أَأَلِدُ وَأَنَا عَجُوزٌ ** 'Shall I bear a child when I am an old woman?' So Allah revealed to him that she would indeed bear a child, but her children would be tormented for four hundred years because of her retort against Me. He said: When the torment became prolonged for the Children of Israel (بني إسرائيل), they cried out and wept to Allah for forty mornings. So Allah revealed to Moses and Aaron that We would deliver them from Pharaoh, and He reduced [the torment] for them by one hundred and seventy years. He said: And Abu Abdillah [Imam al-Sadiq] said: You are like this; if you were to do [as they did], Allah would grant us relief. But if you do not, then the matter will reach its appointed end.

(٧٤) فَلَمَّا ذَهَبَ عَنْ إِبْراهِيمَ الرَّوْعُ ** (74) When the fright left Abraham (i.e., what he sensed of fear, meaning, when his heart was reassured after the fear) وَجاءَتْهُ الْبُشْرى ** and the good news came to him (in place of the fright) يُجادِلُنا فِي قَوْمِ لُوطٍ ** he began to plead with Us concerning the people of Lot. (He pleaded with Our messengers concerning their affair and their purpose (مَعْنَاهُمْ)). And Lot was the son of his maternal aunt, as was previously mentioned in Surah al-A'raf. And his pleading with them was that he said to them: "If there are a hundred believers in it, will you destroy them?" So Gabriel (جبرئيل) said: "No," until the end of what comes in his story.

(٧٥) إِنَّ إِبْراهِيمَ لَحَلِيمٌ ** (75) Indeed, Abraham is forbearing, (He is) not hasty against the one who wronged him with vengeance. Awwāh (أَوَّاهٌ), frequent in supplication. Al-Ayyashi narrated from them (peace be upon them) that they both said: a supplicator (دَعّاءٍ). مُنِيبٌ ** (Munīb) returning to Allah, the Exalted, with what He loves and is pleased with. The purpose of this speech is to explain what motivated him to argue (المجادلة), which is the tenderness of his heart and the excessiveness of his mercy.

(٧٦) يا إِبْراهِيمُ ** (76) O Abraham! [This is] based on the intention of the saying, meaning the angels said: يا إِبْراهِيمُ أَعْرِضْ عَنْ هذا ** O Abraham, disregard this. the argument, and even if mercy is your habit (دأبك), there is no benefit in it. إِنَّهُ قَدْ جاءَ أَمْرُ رَبِّكَ Your Lord’s command has certainly come, His decree (قضاؤه) and His judgment (حُكمه) which is not issued except from wisdom (حكمة). وَإِنَّهُمْ آتِيهِمْ عَذابٌ غَيْرُ مَرْدُودٍ ** and a punishment that cannot be averted will surely overtake them. there is no averting it (لا مردّ له) by argument (بجدال) or anything else.

(٧٧) وَلَمَّا جاءَتْ رُسُلُنا لُوطاً سِيءَ بِهِمْ ** (77) And when Our messengers came to Lot, he was distressed on their account Their coming distressed him because they came in the form of youths (غلمان), and he thought they were humans (أناس), so he feared for them that his people would target them, and he would be unable to defend them. وَضاقَ بِهِمْ ذَرْعاً ** (and he felt straitened on their account) His dhar' (ذرعه) became straitened by their presence, and this is a metaphor (كناية) for the severity of constriction (شِدّة الانقباضِ) due to the inability to ward off the disliked thing (المكروه). وَقالَ هذا يَوْمٌ عَصِيبٌ ** (and he said, 'This is a hard day.') severe (شديد).

(٧٨) وَجاءَهُ قَوْمُهُ يُهْرَعُونَ إِلَيْهِ They were rushing toward him (يُسْرِعُونَ إِلَيْهِ), as if they were being forcefully driven to seek the abomination (الفاحِشة) from his guests. (78) And his people came to him, rushing toward him. وَمِنْ قَبْلُ And before that time (وَمِنْ قَبْلِ ذَلِكَ الْوَقْت), كانُوا يَعْمَلُونَ السَّيِّئاتِ they used to commit the abominations (الفواحش), so they became accustomed to them and were not ashamed of them, until they came rushing toward him openly (مُجَاهِرِينَ). And they had been committing misdeeds before. قالَ يا قَوْمِ هؤُلاءِ بَناتِي "So marry them." He offered them (his daughters) as a ransom for his guests, out of generosity (كَرَماً) and protective zeal (حَمِيَّةً). He said, 'O my people! These are my daughters.

In al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): He offered them marriage (التَّزْوِيج).

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them): He placed his hand on the door, then implored them, saying: "Fear Allah and do not disgrace me concerning my guests," then he offered them his daughters for marriage (بِنِكَاح).

And in al-Qummi, narrated in a disconnected chain (مَقْطُوعاً), he said: He meant their wives, because the Prophet (النَّبِيّ) is the father of his nation (أُمَّتِه), so he called them to what is lawful (الْحَلَال) and was not calling them to what is unlawful (الْحَرَام).

هُنَّ أَطْهَرُ لَكُمْ They are cleaner in action (فِعْلاً) and less obscene (فُحْشاً). It was said that He meant their rear parts (أَدْبَارَهُنَّ). They are purer for you.

In al-Tahdhib and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Rida (peace be upon him): He was asked about a man approaching his wife from behind (مِنْ خَلْفِهَا). He said: "A verse from the Book of Allah (كِتَابِ اللهِ) has made it lawful (أَحَلَّهُ), which is the saying of Lot: هؤُلاءِ بَناتِي هُنَّ أَطْهَرُ لَكُمْ (These are my daughters. They are purer for you)." And He knew that they did not desire the vagina (الْفَرْج).

فَاتَّقُوا اللهَ Regarding the act of engaging with males (فِي مُوَاقَعَةِ الذُّكُور). So be wary of Allah, وَلا تُخْزُونِ And do not make me ashamed (وَلَا تُخْجِلُونِي)—from al-khazāyah (الْخَزَايَة) meaning shyness (الْحَيَاءِ)—or do not expose me (وَلَا تَفْضَحُونِي)—from al-khizy (الْخِزْي)— and do not disgrace me فِي ضَيْفِي concerning my guests (فِي ضَيْفِي), regarding their matter, for disgracing a man's guest is disgracing him. concerning my guests. أَلَيْسَ مِنْكُمْ رَجُلٌ رَشِيدٌ Who is guided to the truth (الْحَقّ) and refrains from the repulsive deed (الْقَبِيح). Is there not a single right-minded man among you?'

(٧٩) قالُوا لَقَدْ عَلِمْتَ ما لَنا فِي بَناتِكَ مِنْ حَقٍ ** (They said, 'You certainly know that we have no right to your daughters,) meaning: no need (حاجة). وَإِنَّكَ لَتَعْلَمُ ما نُرِيدُ ** (and you certainly know what we want.') They intended the act of coming unto males (إتيان الذّكران).

(٨٠) قالَ لَوْ أَنَّ لِي بِكُمْ قُوَّةً ** (He said, 'If only I had the strength against you,) meaning: If I were strong enough by myself to repel you. أَوْ آوِي إِلى رُكْنٍ شَدِيدٍ ** (or could take refuge in a strong support!') or if I could take refuge in a powerful one by whom I could be protected from you, I would certainly repel you from my guests. He likened the mighty and powerful one to the corner (الرّكن) of a mountain in its strength and impregnability. In al-Jawami', Gabriel said: "Indeed, your support (ركنك) is strong. Open the door and leave them to us."

In al-Majma', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "If only he knew what strength He had." And narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "May Allah have mercy upon my brother Lot; he was taking refuge in رُكْنٍ شَدِيدٍ. (a strong support.)"

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "May Allah have mercy upon Lot. If he had known who was with him in the chamber, he would have known that he was victorious, when he said: لَوْ أَنَّ لِي بِكُمْ قُوَّةً أَوْ آوِي إِلى رُكْنٍ شَدِيدٍ ** (If only I had the strength against you, or could take refuge in a strong support!) What support (ركن) is stronger than Gabriel, who was with him in the chamber?"

(81) قالُوا يا لُوطُ إِنَّا رُسُلُ رَبِّكَ ** (81) They said, 'O Lot! We are the apostles of your Lord.' We have been sent for their destruction, so do not grieve. لَنْ يَصِلُوا إِلَيْكَ ** 'They shall not touch you [with any harm].' with any evil, ever. فَأَسْرِ بِأَهْلِكَ ** 'So set out with your family' from الإسراء (al-Isrāʾ), which is traveling by night. وقرئ (Wa quriʾa) with the waṣl (الوصل) from السرّى (as-Surā), which has the same meaning. بِقِطْعٍ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ ** 'in a watch of the night.' meaning, with a portion of it.

Al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding بِقِطْعٍ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ (in a watch of the night): "Darkened (مظلماً)." He said: "This is the reading (قراءة) of the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him)." وَلا يَلْتَفِتْ مِنْكُمْ أَحَدٌ ** 'and none of you shall look back,' meaning, none shall lag behind, or none shall look behind him, إِلَّا امْرَأَتَكَ ** 'except your wife.' And it was read (وقرئ) with the rafʿ (الرفع) (nominative case). إِنَّهُ مُصِيبُها ما أَصابَهُمْ إِنَّ مَوْعِدَهُمُ الصُّبْحُ أَلَيْسَ الصُّبْحُ بِقَرِيبٍ 'Indeed, she will be afflicted by what afflicts them. Indeed, their tryst is the morning. Is the morning not near?'

This is a response to Lot's impatience and his considering the punishment slow. In al-Jawamiʿ, it is narrated that he said: "When is the appointed time for their destruction?" They said: "The morning (الصُّبْحُ)." He said: "I desire something faster than that," due to the distress in his heart concerning them. So they said: أَلَيْسَ الصُّبْحُ بِقَرِيبٍ. 'Is the morning not near?'

In al-ʿIlal and al-Ayyashi, narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Regarding فَأَسْرِ بِأَهْلِكَ ** (So set out with your family), (O Lot), when seven days and nights have passed for you from this day of yours, بِقِطْعٍ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ** (in a watch of the night) means when half the night has passed.

He said: When the eighth day arrived, along with the rising of the dawn, Allah sent forth messengers to Abraham, giving him the good news of Isaac and consoling him (1) regarding the destruction of Lot's people. That is the Almighty's saying: وَلَقَدْ جاءَتْ رُسُلُنا إِبْراهِيمَ بِالْبُشْرى. 'Certainly Our messengers came to Abraham with the good news.'

(٨٢) فَلَمَّا جاءَ أَمْرُنا جَعَلْنا عالِيَها سافِلَها (82) So when Our command came, We turned it upside down, by making Gabriel (جبرئيل) place his wing beneath it, then raising it to the sky, and then turning it over upon them. And the stones followed them from above: وَأَمْطَرْنا عَلَيْهِمْ حِجارَةً مِنْ سِجِّيلٍ (and rained upon them stones of baked clay,) meaning, of petrified clay (طين متحجّر). It is an Arabized word from sank kal (سَنْك كَل), evidenced by the Almighty's saying: حِجارَةً مِنْ طِينٍ مَنْضُودٍ (stones of layered clay,) layered, prepared for their punishment, or He sent some of it following others, consecutively.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: meaning, some of them layered upon others.

مُسَوَّمَةً (marked) marked for punishment.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) said: meaning, dotted. عِنْدَ رَبِّكَ (with your Lord.) in His treasuries. وَما هِيَ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ بِبَعِيدٍ (And they are not far from the wrongdoers.) For they, due to their wrongdoing (ظلم), are deserving that it be rained upon them. It is narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that He asked Gabriel (جبرئيل) about it, and He said: "It means the wrongdoers (ظالمي) of your Ummah (أمّتك). There is no wrongdoer among them except that he is exposed to a stone that falls upon him from hour to hour."

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام: وَما هِيَ مِنَ الظَّالِمِينَ بِبَعِيدٍ (And they are not far from the wrongdoers) refers to the wrongdoers (ظالمي) of your Ummah (أمّتك) if they commit what the people of Lot (قوم لوط) committed.

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: "Whoever dies persisting in sodomy (اللّواط) will not die until Allah strikes him with a stone from those stones, and his death will be in it." And al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ) added: "And no one sees it."

And al-Qummi (القمّيّ), narrated from him عليه السلام: "There is no servant who departs from the world deeming the act of the people of Lot (قوم لوط) lawful, except that Allah strikes his liver with one of those stones, and his death is in it, but the creation does not see it."

And al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from him عليه السلام, from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "When the people of Lot (قوم لوط) committed what they committed, the earth wept to its Lord until its tears reached the Throne (العرش). So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, revealed to the sky to stone them (احْصِبهم) and revealed to the earth to swallow them (اخسفي بهم)."

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir عليه السلام, He said: The people of Lot (قوم لوط) were among the best people Allah created. Iblis (إبليس) sought them out intensely. Part of their virtue and excellence was that when they went out to work, they all went out together, and the women remained behind them. Iblis (إبليس) continued to frequent them, and when they returned, Iblis (إبليس) would destroy what they had been working on. Some of them said to others: "Let us lie in wait for this one who is destroying our belongings." So they lay in wait for him, and behold, he was a boy, the most handsome of boys. They said to him: "Are you the one who destroys our belongings time after time?" They agreed to kill him, so they lodged him with a man for the night. When night came, the boy cried out. The man asked him: "What is wrong with you?" He replied: "My father used to put me to sleep on his belly." The man said to him: "Come, sleep on my belly." He said: The man continued to rub the boy until he taught him to do the act himself. So, first Iblis (إبليس) taught him, and the second time, the man taught him. Then Iblis (إبليس) slipped away and fled from them. When they awoke, the man began to tell what he had done with the boy, and they were amazed by it, though they did not know him. So they began to lay their hands on it until the men were satisfied with men, one with another. Then they began to lie in wait for passersby on the road and commit the act with them until people avoided (1) their city. Then they abandoned their women and turned to the boys. When He saw that He had established His command among the men, He came to the women and transformed Himself into a woman. Then He said: "Your men are committing acts with one another." They said: "Yes, we have seen that." All the while, Lot (لوط) was admonishing them and advising them, and Iblis (إبليس) was leading them astray until the women became satisfied with women. When the proof (الحجّة) was completed against them, Allah sent Gabriel (جبرئيل), Michael (ميكائيل), and Israfil (إسرافيل) in the guise of boys wearing cloaks (أقبية). They passed by Lot (لوط) while he was plowing. He said: "Where are you going? I have never seen anyone more beautiful than you." They said: "Our Master has sent us to the Lord of this city." He said: "Has your Master not been informed of what the people of this city do? O my sons (يا بَنِيَّ), by Allah, they seize men and commit acts with them until blood flows." They said: "Our Master commanded us to pass through the middle of it." He said: "I have a request of you." They asked: "What is it?" He said: "Wait here until darkness falls." So they sat down. He said: Then he sent his daughter, saying: "Bring them bread, and bring them water in the gourd (1), and bring them a cloak (عباء) to cover themselves from the cold." When the daughter left, the rain and the valley approached. Lot (لوط) said: "The valley will carry the boys away now." He said: "Get up so we may leave." Lot (لوط) began to walk along the base of the wall, while Gabriel (جبرئيل), Michael (ميكائيل), and Israfil (إسرافيل) walked in the middle of the road. He said: "O my sons (يا بَنِيَّ), walk here." They said: "Our Master commanded us to pass through the middle of it." Lot (لوط) was taking advantage of the darkness. Iblis (إبليس) passed by and took a boy from a woman's lap and threw him into the well. Then all the people of the city cried out at Lot's (لوط) door. When they saw the boys in Lot's (لوط) house, they said: "O Lot (لوط), you have joined our practice!" He said: "These are my guests, so do not disgrace me concerning my guests." They said: "They are three; take one and give us two." He said: And he brought them into the chamber. Lot (لوط) said: "If only I had a household (أهل بيت) to protect me from you!" He said: They pushed against the door and broke Lot's (لوط) door and threw Lot (لوط) down. Then Gabriel (جبرئيل) said to him: إِنَّا رُسُلُ رَبِّكَ لَنْ يَصِلُوا إِلَيْكَ (We are the messengers of your Lord; they shall not reach you.) So he took a handful of pebbles (2) and struck their faces with it, saying: "May the faces be deformed (شاهت الوجوه)." Then all the people of the city became blind. Lot (لوط) said to them: "O messengers of my Lord, what has my Lord commanded you concerning them?" They said: "We have been commanded to seize them at dawn (السّحر)." He said: "I have a request of you." They asked: "What is your request?" He said: "Seize them now, for I fear that my Lord may change His mind concerning them." They said: "O Lot (لوط), إِنَّ مَوْعِدَهُمُ الصُّبْحُ أَلَيْسَ الصُّبْحُ بِقَرِيبٍ (Indeed, their tryst is the morning. Is the morning not near?) for the one who intends to seize. So take your daughters and depart, and leave your wife."

And in it (al-Kafi) and al-Ayyashi (العيّاشيّ), narrated from al-Sadiq عليه السلام: Allah sent four angels for the destruction of the people of Lot (قوم لوط): Gabriel (جبرئيل), Michael (ميكائيل), Israfil (إسرافيل), and Karubil (كروبيل). They passed by Abraham (إبراهيم) while they were veiled (معتمّون). They greeted him, but he did not recognize them. He saw their handsome appearance and said: "No one shall serve these except myself." He was a host, so he roasted a fat calf for them until it was well-done, then brought it near them. When he placed it before them, رَأَى أَيْدِيَهُمْ لا تَصِلُ إِلَيْهِ نَكِرَهُمْ وَأَوْجَسَ مِنْهُمْ خِيفَةً (he saw that their hands did not reach for it, he took them for strangers and conceived a fear of them). When Gabriel (جبرئيل) saw that, he uncovered the turban from his face and head, and Abraham (إبراهيم) recognized him and said: "Are you him?" He said: "Yes." And Sarah (سارة), his wife, passed by, and they gave her the good news of Isaac (إسحق), وَمِنْ وَراءِ إِسْحاقَ يَعْقُوبَ (and after Isaac, Jacob). She said what Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, said, and they answered her with what is in the Glorious Book (الكتاب العزيز). Abraham (إبراهيم) said to them: "Why have you come?" They said: "For the destruction of the people of Lot (قوم لوط)." He said to them: "If there are one hundred believers in it, will you destroy them?" Gabriel (جبرئيل) said: "No." He said: "What if there are fifty?" He said: "No." He said: "What if there are thirty?" He said: "No." He said: "What if there are twenty?" He said: "No." He said: "What if there are ten?" He said: "No." He said: "What if there are five?" He said: "No." He said: "What if there is one?" He said: "No." He said: فَإِنَّ فِيها لُوطاً (But Lot is in it). They said: قَالُوا نَحْنُ أَعْلَمُ بِمَنْ فِيهَا لَنُنَجِّيَنَّهُ وَأَهْلَهُ إِلَّا امْرَأَتَهُ كَانَتْ مِنَ الْغَابِرِينَ (They said, 'We know well who is in it. We will surely save him and his family, except his wife; she is one of those who stay behind.') The narrator said: I do not know this saying except that he was seeking to spare them, and this is the saying of Allah: يُجَادِلُنَا فِي قَوْمِ لُوطٍ (He pleaded with Us concerning the people of Lot). So they came to Lot (لوط) while he was in a field near the village. They greeted him while they were veiled (معتمّون). When he saw their handsome appearance, wearing white clothes and white turbans, he offered them lodging. They said: "Yes." So he walked ahead of them, and they walked behind him. He regretted offering them lodging, saying: "What have I done? I am bringing them to my people, and I know them." He turned to them and said: "You are coming to the worst of Allah's creation." The Almighty said to Gabriel (جبرئيل): "Do not hasten against them until he testifies against them three times." Gabriel (جبرئيل) said: "This is one." Then he walked for a while, then turned to them and said: "You are coming to the worst of Allah's creation." Gabriel (جبرئيل) said: "This is two." Then he walked. When he reached the city gate, he turned to them and said: "You are coming to the worst of Allah's creation." Gabriel (جبرئيل) said: "This is the third." Then he entered, and they entered with him until he reached his house. When his wife saw them, she saw their handsome appearance, so she climbed onto the roof and clapped, but they did not hear. So she made smoke (دخّنت). When they saw the smoke, they rushed forward until they reached the door. She came down to them and said: "He has guests; I have never seen people more handsome in appearance than them." So they came to the door to enter. When Lot (لوط) saw them, he stood up to them and said: "O my people, فَاتَّقُوا اللَّهَ وَلَا تُخْزُونِ فِي ضَيْفِي أَلَيْسَ مِنْكُمْ رَجُلٌ رَشِيدٌ (So be wary of Allah, and do not disgrace me concerning my guests. Is there not a single right-minded man among you?) And he said: هَؤُلَاءِ بَنَاتِي هُنَّ أَطْهَرُ لَكُمْ (These are my daughters; they are purer for you.) Thus, he invited them to the lawful. They said: قَالُوا لَقَدْ عَلِمْتَ مَا لَنَا فِي بَنَاتِكَ مِنْ حَقٍّ وَإِنَّكَ لَتَعْلَمُ مَا نُرِيدُ قَالَ لَوْ أَنَّ لِي بِكُمْ قُوَّةً أَوْ آوِي إِلَى رُكْنٍ شَدِيدٍ (They said, 'You certainly know that we have no right to your daughters, and you surely know what we want.') Gabriel (جبرئيل) said: "If only he knew what strength He had!" He said: They overwhelmed him until they entered the house. Gabriel (جبرئيل) shouted at them and said: "O Lot (لوط), let them enter." When they entered, Gabriel (جبرئيل) pointed his finger towards them, and their eyes were blinded. This is the saying of Allah: فَطَمَسْنَا أَعْيُنَهُمْ (Then We blotted out their eyes). Then Gabriel (جبرئيل) called out to him and said: إِنَّا رُسُلُ رَبِّكَ لَنْ يَصِلُوا إِلَيْكَ فَأَسْرِ بِأَهْلِكَ بِقِطْعٍ مِنَ اللَّيْلِ (We are the messengers of your Lord; they shall not reach you. So set out with your family in a watch of the night). And Gabriel (جبرئيل) said to him: "We have been sent for their destruction." Lot (لوط) said: "O Gabriel (جبرئيل), hasten!" He said: إِنَّ مَوْعِدَهُمُ الصُّبْحُ أَلَيْسَ الصُّبْحُ بِقَرِيبٍ (Indeed, their tryst is the morning. Is the morning not near?) So He commanded him to take himself and those with him, except his wife. Then Gabriel (جبرئيل) uprooted it—meaning the city—with his wing from seven layers of earth, then raised it until the inhabitants of the lowest heaven heard the barking of the dogs and the crowing of the roosters. Then He turned it over and rained upon it and upon those around the city stones of sijjil (سجيل).

I say: A portion of the story of the people of Lot (قوم لوط) has already preceded in Surat al-A'raf (سورة الأعراف), and another part of it will come in Surat al-Hijr (سورة الحجر), if Allah wills.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ) mentioned the story of Abraham (إبراهيم) and Lot (لوط) with an extensive explanation, without attribution (اسناد) to an infallible (معصوم), containing things other than what we have mentioned. Whoever desires it should refer back to it.

(84) وَإِلى مَدْيَنَ أَخاهُمْ شُعَيْباً قالَ يا قَوْمِ اعْبُدُوا اللهَ ما لَكُمْ مِنْ إِلهٍ غَيْرُهُ ** (84) And to [the people of] Midian [We sent] Shu‘ayb, their brother. He said, ‘O my people, worship Allah! You have no god other than Him. Its exegesis has already passed in Sūrat al-A‘rāf. وَلا تَنْقُصُوا الْمِكْيالَ وَالْمِيزانَ إِنِّي أَراكُمْ بِخَيْرٍ ** Do not diminish the measure and the balance. Indeed, I see you to be well-off, [meaning] with an abundance (بِسَعَةٍ) that makes you independent of cheating (الْبَخْس). In al-Faqih and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: "Their price (سِعْر) was cheap (رَخِيص)." وَإِنِّي أَخافُ عَلَيْكُمْ عَذابَ يَوْمٍ مُحِيطٍ ** and indeed I fear for you the punishment of an all-encompassing day. [meaning] destructive (مُهْلِك), derived from His saying: "and his fruit was encompassed (وَأُحِيطَ بِثَمَرِهِ)" [referring to destruction], or [meaning] that none of you will escape (لَا يَشُذّ) from it.

(٨٥) وَيا قَوْمِ أَوْفُوا الْمِكْيالَ وَالْمِيزانَ ** (85) 'O my people! Fill the measure and the balance completely The command to fulfill (the measure and balance) was explicitly stated after the prohibition of its opposite, as an emphasis and a warning that it is not sufficient for them merely to refrain from intentionally giving short measure (التطفيف); rather, it is incumbent upon them to strive for fulfillment, even if it requires a surplus (بزيادة) without which it cannot be achieved. بِالْقِسْطِ ** with justice, meaning: with fairness (بالعدل) and equality (والسوية).

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "We found in the book of the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم that if they give short measure in the measure (المكيال) and the balance (الميزان), Allah will seize them with years of drought (بالسنين) and deficiency (والنقص)." And in another narration: "and severity of provision (وشدة المؤنة) and the tyranny of the ruler (وجور السلطان)."

وَلا تَبْخَسُوا النَّاسَ أَشْياءَهُمْ ** and do not diminish people's goods, This is a generalization following a specification, for it is more general than being related to quantity (المقدار) or to anything else. وَلا تَعْثَوْا فِي الْأَرْضِ مُفْسِدِينَ ** and do not act corruptly on the earth as mischief-makers. This is also a generalization following a specification, for corruption (العثو) encompasses the diminution of rights (تنقيص الحقوق) and other types of corruption (الفساد), such as theft (السرقة), raiding (الغارة), highway robbery (قطع السبل), and other such things.

(86) بَقِيَّتُ اللهِ ** What He has left for you of the lawful (الحلال) after abstaining (التنزّه) from what is unlawful (حرام) خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ ** than what you gather through short-changing (التطفيف) إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ ** due to the condition of faith (الإيمان), since the reward (الثواب) and salvation (النجاة) from punishment (العقاب) are not attained except through it. Or He means: if you are believing me (مصدقين لي) in my advice (نصيحتي). وَما أَنَا عَلَيْكُمْ بِحَفِيظٍ ** guarding your deeds for you.

(86) **بَقِيَّتُ اللهِ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ وَما أَنَا عَلَيْكُمْ بِحَفِيظٍ ** (86) What is left by Allah is better for you, should you be faithful, and I am not a guardian over you.

In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): He ascended a mountain overlooking the people of Madyan when the gate of Madyan was closed against him and he was prevented from going out to them in the marketplaces. So he addressed them with his loudest voice: "O people of the city whose inhabitants are unjust! I am the Remainder of Allah (بقيّة الله)." Allah says: بَقِيَّتُ اللهِ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ وَما أَنَا عَلَيْكُمْ بِحَفِيظٍ ** (86) What is left by Allah is better for you, should you be faithful, and I am not a guardian over you. He said: There was an old man (شيخ كبير) among them, who came to them and said: "O my people, by Allah, this is the call (دعوة) of Shu'ayb the Prophet (peace be upon him)! By Allah, if you do not go out to this man in the marketplaces, you will surely be seized from above you and from beneath your feet." (The rest of) the narrative (الحديث).

And in al-Ikmal, narrated from him (peace be upon him): The first thing that al-Qa'im (peace be upon him) will utter when he emerges is this verse: بَقِيَّتُ اللهِ خَيْرٌ لَكُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُمْ مُؤْمِنِينَ (What is left by Allah is better for you, should you be faithful). Then he will say: "I am the Remainder of Allah (بقيّة الله), His Proof (حجّته), and His Vicegerent (خليفته) over you." Thus, no one who greets him (مُسلّم) will greet him except by saying: "Peace be upon you, O Remainder of Allah (بقيّة الله) on His earth."

(87) قالُوا يا شُعَيْبُ أَصَلاتُكَ تَأْمُرُكَ أَنْ نَتْرُكَ ما يَعْبُدُ آباؤُنا ** (They said, 'O Shu‘ayb! Does your prayer bid you that we should abandon what our fathers used to worship) [The phrase] من الأصنام (of the idols) [is implied], meaning: Does it bid you that you should impose that upon us? They responded to his command for monotheism (التوحيد) with mockery (الاستهزاء) of him and sarcasm (التهكم) regarding his prayer, indicating that something like this is not called for by a rational motive (داع عقليّ), but rather, you were called to it by fleeting thoughts (خطرات) and whisperings (وساوس) of the same kind as that which you constantly observe. He (Shu‘ayb) was frequent in prayer (الصلوة), and for that reason, they used the plural and singled it out for mention. And it was read (وقرء) in the singular [form]. أَوْ أَنْ نَفْعَلَ فِي أَمْوالِنا ما نَشؤُا ** (or that we should not do as we wish with our wealth?) [Meaning:] Or that we should abandon our actions concerning our wealth. This is the response to the prohibition against short-changing (التطفيف) and the command for giving full measure (الايفاءِ). إِنَّكَ لَأَنْتَ الْحَلِيمُ الرَّشِيدُ ** (Indeed, you are the forbearing, the right-minded one!) It is said (قيل) that by this, they intended to attribute to him the utmost foolishness (السفه) and error (الغيّ) in order to mock him, so they used the opposite [of what they meant]. And al-Qummi said: They said: "Indeed, you are the foolish (السفيه), the ignorant (الجاهل)." So Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), related their saying, and He said: إِنَّكَ لَأَنْتَ الْحَلِيمُ الرَّشِيدُ.

(٨٨) قالَ يا قَوْمِ أَرَأَيْتُمْ إِنْ كُنْتُ عَلى بَيِّنَةٍ مِنْ رَبِّي ** (88) He said, 'O my people! Tell me, if I stand on a manifest proof from my Lord, قيل إشارة إلى ما آتاه الله من العلم والنبوّة وَرَزَقَنِي مِنْهُ رِزْقاً حَسَناً ** and He has provided me a good provision from Him, قيل إشارة إلى ما آتيه الله من المال الحلال وجواب الشرط محذوف تقديره فهل يسع لي مع هذه الأنعام أن أخون في وحيه وأخالفه في أمره ونهيه ولا آمركم بترك عبادة الأوثان والكف عن القبائح وانّما بعثني لذلك وَما أُرِيدُ أَنْ أُخالِفَكُمْ إِلى ما أَنْهاكُمْ عَنْهُ يعني وَما أُرِيدُ ** أن أسبقكم إلى شهواتكم التي نهيتكم عنها لأستبدّ بها دونكم إِنْ أُرِيدُ إِلَّا الْإِصْلاحَ ** ان أصلحكم مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ ** ما دمت أستطيع الإِصلاح فلو وجدت الصَّلاح فيما أنتم عليه لما نهيتكم عنه والجواب الأوّل إشارة إلى مراعاة حقّ الله والثاني إلى مراعاة حقّ النفس والثالث إلى مراعاة حق الناس وَما تَوْفِيقِي إِلَّا بِاللهِ ** الا بهدايته ومعونته عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ ** فانه القادر المتمكن من كلّ شيء دون غيره وفيه إشارة إلى مخض التوحيد الذي هو أقصى مراتب العلم بالمبدإ وَإِلَيْهِ أُنِيبُ ** إشارة إلى معرفة المعاد نبّه بهذه الكلمات على إقباله على الله بشراشرِه فيما يأتي ويذر وحسم اطماع الكفّار وعدم المبالاة بعداوتهم وتهديدهم بالرجوع إلى الله للجزاء. It was said: This is a reference to the knowledge and prophethood that Allah granted him. It was said: This is a reference to the lawful wealth (المال الحلال) that Allah granted him. And the apodosis (جواب الشرط) is omitted, its estimation (تقديره) being: "Is it then permissible for me, despite these blessings (الأنعام), to betray His revelation, disobey Him in His command and prohibition, and not command you to abandon idol worship (عبادة الأوثان) and refrain from abominations (القبائح), when He only sent me for that purpose? And I do not desire to contravene you concerning what I forbid you from." Meaning: وَما أُرِيدُ ** (And I do not desire) to precede you to your desires (شهواتكم) which I have forbidden you from, so that I may monopolize them instead of you. إِنْ أُرِيدُ إِلَّا الْإِصْلاحَ ** (I desire nothing but reform) that I reform you مَا اسْتَطَعْتُ ** (as far as I can) as long as I am able to perform reform. For if I had found righteousness (الصَّلاح) in what you are upon, I would not have forbidden you from it. And the first answer (الجواب الأوّل) is a reference to observing the right of Allah (حقّ الله), the second to observing the right of the self (حقّ النفس), and the third to observing the right of the people (حق الناس). وَما تَوْفِيقِي إِلَّا بِاللهِ ** (My success lies only with Allah) except through His guidance and His assistance (معونته). عَلَيْهِ تَوَكَّلْتُ ** (In Him I have put my trust) for He is the Capable One (القادر), the One Who has power (المتمكن) over everything, unlike others. And in this is a reference to pure Monotheism (مخض التوحيد), which is the highest rank of knowledge concerning the Originator (المبدإ). وَإِلَيْهِ أُنِيبُ ** (and to Him I turn penitently) a reference to the knowledge of the Return (المعاد). Through these words, he drew attention to his complete devotion (إقباله) to Allah with all his being (بشراشرِه) in what he undertakes and what he leaves, and to cutting off the ambitions (اطماع) of the disbelievers, and to his disregard (عدم المبالاة) for their enmity and their threats, by referring the matter back to Allah for recompense (للجزاء).

(٨٩) وَيا قَوْمِ لا يَجْرِمَنَّكُمْ ** Let it not cause you (لا يكسبنّكم) شِقاقِي ** my disagreement (خلافي) and my enmity (ومعاداتي) أَنْ يُصِيبَكُمْ مِثْلُ ما أَصابَ قَوْمَ نُوحٍ ** that there should befall you the like of what befell the people of Noah, namely, the drowning (من الغرق) أَوْ قَوْمَ هُودٍ ** or the people of Hud, namely, the wind (من الريح) أَوْ قَوْمَ صالِحٍ ** or the people of Salih, namely, the tremor (من الرّجفة) وَما قَوْمُ لُوطٍ مِنْكُمْ بِبَعِيدٍ ٨٩ And O my people! Let not your opposition to me incite you to a sin, lest there should befall you the like of what befell the people of Noah, or the people of Hud, or the people of Salih. And the people of Lot are not far from you.** (89)

meaning that they were destroyed in a time close to your time. So if you do not take heed by those before them, then take heed by them.

(٩٠) وَاسْتَغْفِرُوا رَبَّكُمْ ثُمَّ تُوبُوا إِلَيْهِ ** concerning what you are upon. إِنَّ رَبِّي رَحِيمٌ وَدُودٌ ** (90) And plead with your Lord for forgiveness, then turn to Him penitently. Indeed, my Lord is all-merciful, all-loving. great in mercy, showing affection (متودّد) towards His servants, desiring their benefits. And this is a promise regarding repentance, following the threat concerning persistence (in sin).

(٩١) قالُوا يا شُعَيْبُ ما نَفْقَهُ ** They said, 'O Shu'ayb, we do not understand what we understand كَثِيراً مِمَّا تَقُولُ وَإِنَّا لَنَراكَ فِينا ضَعِيفاً ** much of what you say, and indeed we see you among us as weak. You have neither strength nor honor (عزّ), so you would not be able to prevent us (from harming you) if we intended harm for you.

Al-Qummi (القمّيّ), and his eyesight had become weak: وَلَوْ لا رَهْطُكَ ** Were it not for your clan, your people, and their honor (عزّتهم) with us because they are upon our religion, لَرَجَمْناكَ ** we would stone you, meaning, we would kill you the worst kind of killing. وَما أَنْتَ عَلَيْنا بِعَزِيزٍ ** and you are not mighty against us.' so that your might (عزّتك) would prevent us from killing you. Rather, your clan (رهطك) are the ones who are mighty (الاعزّة) to us.

(٩٢) قالَ يا قَوْمِ أَرَهْطِي أَعَزُّ عَلَيْكُمْ مِنَ اللهِ وَاتَّخَذْتُمُوهُ وَراءَكُمْ ظِهْرِيًّا ** (92) He said, 'O my people! Is my clan dearer to you than Allah, whom you have taken lightly, casting Him behind your backs? And you have made Him like the forgotten, cast aside behind the back, who is not cared for. And الظهري (al-ẓihrī) is attributed to the back (الظهر), and the kasrah is one of the changes (تغييرات) of attribution (النسب). إِنَّ رَبِّي بِما تَعْمَلُونَ مُحِيطٌ ** Indeed my Lord comprehends what you do. So nothing of it is hidden from Him.

(٩٣) وَيا قَوْمِ اعْمَلُوا عَلى مَكانَتِكُمْ ** (93) And O my people, act according to your station remaining firm upon what you are upon of polytheism (الشرك) and enmity. إِنِّي عامِلٌ سَوْفَ تَعْلَمُونَ مَنْ يَأْتِيهِ عَذابٌ يُخْزِيهِ وَمَنْ هُوَ كاذِبٌ ** I am acting. Soon you will know whom a humiliating punishment will befall, and who is a liar. Who is the punished one (المعذب) and the liar (الكاذب), from me or from you? A similar instance preceded this in Sūrat al-An‘ām. وَارْتَقِبُوا ** And wait and await what I say to you. إِنِّي مَعَكُمْ رَقِيبٌ ** I am waiting with you. awaiting.

In al-Ikmal and al-Majma‘, narrated from al-Rida (peace be upon him): "How excellent is patience and awaiting the relief (انتظار الفرج)! Have you not heard the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: وَارْتَقِبُوا إِنِّي مَعَكُمْ رَقِيبٌ (And wait; I am waiting with you.)"

And in al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): "Indeed, awaiting the relief (انتظار الفرج) is part of the relief (الفرج)." Then he recited this verse.

And in al-Majma‘, narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Shu‘ayb was the orator (خطيب) of the prophets."

(٩٤) وَلَمَّا جاءَ أَمْرُنا نَجَّيْنا شُعَيْباً وَالَّذِينَ آمَنُوا مَعَهُ بِرَحْمَةٍ مِنَّا ** (94) When Our command came, We delivered Shu‘ayb and those who had believed with him by a mercy from Us. The conjunction waw (و) was mentioned here and in the story of 'Ad, whereas the conjunction fa (ف) was used in the stories of Salih and Hud, due to the prior mention of a promise (وعد) which functions as a cause (السبب) in the stories of Salih and Hud, but not in the other two. وَأَخَذَتِ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا الصَّيْحَةُ And the Cry seized those who were wrongdoers. In al-Jawami', it is narrated: Jibril (جبرئيل) cried out a single cry (صيحة) at them, and the soul (روح) of every single one of them departed right where he was. فَأَصْبَحُوا فِي دِيارِهِمْ جاثِمِينَ.** So they remained in their homes fallen on their faces.

(٩٥) كَأَنْ لَمْ يَغْنَوْا فِيها ** (as if they had never lived in it) as if they had not resided therein alive. أَلا بُعْداً لِمَدْيَنَ كَما بَعِدَتْ ثَمُودُ ** (Away with Midian, just as Thamud was done away with!) It was said that He likened them (Midian) to them (Thamud) because their punishment was also by the Cry (الصيحة), except that their Cry (Thamud's) was from beneath them, and the Cry of Midian was from above them.

(٩٦) وَلَقَدْ أَرْسَلْنا مُوسى بِآياتِنا وَسُلْطانٍ مُبِينٍ ** (96) Certainly We sent Moses with Our signs and a manifest authority. with the overwhelming miracles (المعجزات القاهرة) and the dazzling proofs (الحُجَج الباهرة). (٩٧) إِلى فِرْعَوْنَ وَمَلَائِهِ فَاتَّبَعُوا أَمْرَ فِرْعَوْنَ ** (97) to Pharaoh and his elite. But they followed Pharaoh’s command. (His command was) his command to disbelieve in Moses. وَما أَمْرُ فِرْعَوْنَ بِرَشِيدٍ ** And Pharaoh’s command was not right-minded. There is no guidance (رشدٍ) in his command; rather, it is error (غيّ) and misguidance (ضَلال). (٩٨) يَقْدُمُ قَوْمَهُ يَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ** (98) He will lead his people on the Day of Resurrection, By leading them forward to the Fire, and they will follow him, just as he was an example (قدوة) for them in misguidance (الضّلال) in the world. فَأَوْرَدَهُمُ النَّارَ ** and bring them to the Fire. He mentioned it using the past tense (بلفظ الماضي) to emphasize the certainty of its occurrence. وَبِئْسَ الْوِرْدُ الْمَوْرُودُ ** And evil is the arrival they arrive at! Evil is the arrival (الورد) which they arrive at: the Fire. This is because the arrival (الْوِرْدُ), which is the water that is arrived at, is only sought to quench thirst (تسكين العطش) and cool the livers (تبريد الأكباد), whereas the Fire is its opposite.

(٩٩) وَأُتْبِعُوا فِي هذِهِ الدنيا لَعْنَةً وَيَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ** (99) (And they were followed in this [world] by a curse, and on the Day of Resurrection.) meaning, they are cursed in this world and the Hereafter. How evil is the aid (بِئْسَ الرِّفْدُ الْمَرْفُودُ) that was given to them, because الرِّفْدُ , which is assistance (العون) and giving (العطاء), is only intended for benefit (النفع), whereas the curse (اللعنة) brings forth punishment (العذاب) in both abodes (الدارين). Al-Qummi (said): Regarding فِي هذِهِ لَعْنَةً ** (in this [world] by a curse), He means destruction (الهلاك) and drowning (الغرق). And regarding وَيَوْمَ الْقِيامَةِ ** (and on the Day of Resurrection), Allah aided (رفدهم) them with punishment (العذاب).

(١٠٠) ذلِكَ مِنْ أَنْباءِ الْقُرى نَقُصُّهُ عَلَيْكَ مِنْها قائِمٌ وَحَصِيدٌ (That is part of the accounts of the towns which We recount to you. Of them some are standing, and some mown down.) meaning that news مِنْ أَنْباءِ الْقُرى ** the destroyed ones, which We recount to you. مِنْها قائِمٌ ** [meaning] remaining, like the standing crop (الزّرع القائِم), وَحَصِيدٌ ** (and some mown down). And some of them have their traces obliterated (عافي الأثر), like the mown crop (الزّرع المحصود).

And al-'Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): "It was only read فَمِنْها قائِماً وَحَصِيداً with the accusative case (بالنصب). He said: 'The mown down (الحصيد) is only achieved by iron (بالحديد) (1).'"

(١٠١) وَما ظَلَمْناهُمْ ** (101) We did not wrong them, by Our destruction of them. وَلكِنْ ظَلَمُوا أَنْفُسَهُمْ ** but they wronged themselves by exposing them (their souls) to it through committing that which necessitates it. فَما أَغْنَتْ عَنْهُمْ ** So their gods whom they invoked besides Allah did not avail them in any wise So they did not benefit them, nor were they able to repel from them their gods whom they invoke besides Allah in any wise آلِهَتُهُمُ الَّتِي يَدْعُونَ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ مِنْ شَيْءٍ لَمَّا جاءَ أَمْرُ رَبِّكَ ** when the command of your Lord came, meaning His punishment (عذابه) and His vengeance (نقمته). وَما زادُوهُمْ غَيْرَ تَتْبِيبٍ ** and they added nothing to them except ruin. except loss (تخسير).

(102) وَكَذلِكَ ** And similar to that seizing أَخْذُ رَبِّكَ إِذا أَخَذَ الْقُرى ** meaning, their people وَهِيَ ظالِمَةٌ إِنَّ أَخْذَهُ أَلِيمٌ شَدِيدٌ ** grievous, difficult. (102) And thus is the seizing of your Lord when He seizes the towns while they are doing wrong. Indeed, His seizing is painful and severe.**

In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: "Indeed, Allah grants respite to the wrongdoer until, when He seizes him, He does not let him escape." Then he recited this Ayah.

footnote (١) أي لا يكون الّا بالسّيف والرّمح والسّهم ونحو ذلك والظاهر أنّه مستأنف منفصل عما قبله لا انه علّة لقراءة النّصب بل بيان المراد مرفوعاً أو منصوباً وعلى تقدير النّصب يكون التقدير منها أبقى قائماً ومنها احصد حصيداً على انّهما حالان مؤكد ان مثل زيد أبوك عطوفاً. (1) Meaning, it does not occur except by the sword (السّيف), the spear (الرّمح), the arrow (السّهم), and the like. And it is apparent that it is a new sentence (مستأنف), separate from what precedes it, not that it is the reason (علّة) for the reading with the nasb (النّصب) (accusative case). Rather, it is a clarification of the intended meaning, whether it is in the marfu' (nominative) or mansub (accusative) case. And based on the assumption of nasb (النّصب), the implied meaning (التقدير) would be: "from them [is] remaining standing" and "from them [is] harvested as a harvest," on the basis that they are two emphasizing circumstantial qualifiers (حالان مؤكد ان), similar to [the example] Zayd is your father, being compassionate (زيد أبوك عطوفاً).

(١٠٣) إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ ** (Indeed, in that) means: in what descended upon the perished nations. لَآيَةً ** (there is indeed a sign) means: a lesson (لعبرة). لِمَنْ خافَ عَذابَ الْآخِرَةِ ** (for him who fears the punishment of the Hereafter) means: due to his knowledge that it is a sample (أنموذج) of it. That (ذلِكَ) means: the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة) and the punishment of the Hereafter (عذاب الآخرة). يَوْمٌ مَجْمُوعٌ لَهُ النَّاسُ ** (a day on which mankind will be gathered) means: the former ones (الأوّلون) and the latter ones (الآخِرون). وَذلِكَ يَوْمٌ مَشْهُودٌ (and that is a day which will be witnessed) means: many have witnessed it (كثير شاهدوه).

إِنَّ فِي ذلِكَ لَآيَةً لِمَنْ خافَ عَذابَ الْآخِرَةِ (103) ذلِكَ يَوْمٌ مَجْمُوعٌ لَهُ النَّاسُ وَذلِكَ يَوْمٌ مَشْهُودٌ (104) (103) There is indeed a sign in that for him who fears the punishment of the Hereafter. (104) That is a day on which mankind will be gathered, and that is a day which will be witnessed.

Al-Qummi (said): The Prophets (الأنبياء) and the Messengers (الرّسل) will bear witness to it. And it was said: مَشْهُودٌ (witnessed) means that the inhabitants of the heavens (السماوات) and the earths (الأرضين) will be present therein.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from one of the two Imams (peace be upon them both) (أحدهما عليهما السلام) regarding this verse: That is the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة), and it is the Promised Day (اليوم الموعود).

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sajjad (peace be upon him) (السّجّاد) in a discourse of his concerning admonitions (المواعظ) and asceticism (الزّهد): "And know that beyond this is something greater, more terrible, and more painful for the hearts: the Day of Resurrection (يوم القيامة). ذلِكَ يَوْمٌ مَجْمُوعٌ لَهُ النَّاسُ وَذلِكَ يَوْمٌ مَشْهُودٌ ** (That is a day on which mankind will be gathered, and that is a day which will be witnessed). Allah, the Mighty and Majestic (عزّ وجلّ), will gather therein the former ones (الأوّلينَ) and the latter ones (الآخِرين)."

(١٠٤) وَما نُؤَخِّرُهُ ** (104) And We do not defer it except until a numbered term. meaning, the Day (of Judgment) إِلَّا لِأَجَلٍ مَعْدُودٍ ** except until the end of a numbered, finite period. (١٠٥) يَوْمَ يَأْتِ ** (105) When that day comes, no soul shall speak except by His leave. Among them will be the wretched and the felicitous. And it was read (وقرئ) with the omission of the yā’ (الياء) لا تَكَلَّمُ ** meaning, لَا تَتَكَلَّمُ (shall not speak) نَفْسٌ ** with what benefits or saves it إِلَّا بِإِذْنِهِ ** except by His leave, by the leave of Allah, like His saying: لَا يَتَكَلَّمُونَ إِلَّا مَنْ أَذِنَ لَهُ الرَّحْمَنُ ** (They shall not speak, except him whom the All-beneficent permits) and He is pleased with his speech. This is in one station (مَوْطِن) among the stations of that Day. And His saying: هَذَا يَوْمُ لَا يَنْطِقُونَ وَلَا يُؤْذَنُ لَهُمْ فَيَعْتَذِرُونَ ** (This is a day when they will not speak, nor will they be permitted to make excuses) is in other stations (مَوَاطِن أُخَر) of it. Among them is as in al-Tawhid, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): فَمِنْهُمْ شَقِيٌّ وَسَعِيدٌ (Among them will be the wretched and the felicitous). So, among them is the wretched (شَقِيٌّ) for whom the Fire is necessitated (وَجَبَتْ) according to the requirement of the threat (الوعيد), and the felicitous (سَعِيدٌ) for whom Paradise is necessitated (وَجَبَتْ) according to the requirement of the promise (الوعد).

(١٠٦) فَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ شَقُوا فَفِي النَّارِ لَهُمْ فِيها زَفِيرٌ وَشَهِيقٌ ** (106) But as for those who are wretched, they will be in the Fire. There will be sighing and groaning for them in it. Az-Zafir (الزّفير) is the expulsion of the breath, and ash-Shahīq (الشهيق) is its return. By these two, He indicates the severity of their distress and grief. (١٠٧) خالِدِينَ فِيها ما دامَتِ السَّماواتُ وَالْأَرْضُ إِلَّا ما شاءَ رَبُّكَ إِنَّ رَبَّكَ فَعَّالٌ لِما يُرِيدُ. (107) They will remain in it as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except what your Lord wills. Indeed your Lord is the doer of what He wills. (١٠٨) وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ سُعِدُوا فَفِي الْجَنَّةِ خالِدِينَ فِيها ما دامَتِ السَّماواتُ وَالْأَرْضُ إِلَّا ما شاءَ رَبُّكَ عَطاءً غَيْرَ مَجْذُوذٍ ** (108) But as for those who are felicitous, they will be in paradise, remaining in it as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except what your Lord wills—an unending gift. Uninterrupted (غير مقطوع).

Al-Qummi said regarding this Ayah يَوْمَ يَأْتِ ** (the day it comes) and the one after it: "This refers to the Fire of the world (نار الدنيا) before the Day of Resurrection." He said: "As for His saying: وَأَمَّا الَّذِينَ سُعِدُوا فَفِي الْجَنَّةِ خالِدِينَ فِيها ** (But as for those who are felicitous, they will be in paradise, remaining in it), it means in the gardens (جنان) of the world (الدّنيا) to which the souls of the believers are transferred. ما دامَتِ السَّماواتُ وَالْأَرْضُ إِلَّا ما شاءَ رَبُّكَ عَطاءً غَيْرَ مَجْذُوذٍ ** (as long as the heavens and the earth endure, except what your Lord wills—an unending gift), meaning uninterrupted (غير مقطوع) from the bliss of the Hereafter in Paradise, which will be connected to it." He said: "And this is a refutation against those who deny the torment of the grave (عذاب القبر), and the reward and punishment in the world (الدّنيا) and the intermediate realm (البرزخ) before the Day of Resurrection."

I say: This interpretation is supported by the Almighty's saying: النَّارُ يُعْرَضُونَ عَلَيْها غُدُوًّا وَعَشِيًّا. (The Fire, they are exposed to it morning and evening.) Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "Indeed, this refers to the Fire of the intermediate realm (نار البرزخ) before the Day of Resurrection, since there is neither morning (غدوّ) nor evening (عشيّ) on the Day of Resurrection."
