Then He (peace be upon him) said: "Have you not heard the saying of Allah, the Mighty and Majestic: يَوْمَ تَقُومُ السَّاعَةُ أَدْخِلُوا آلَ فِرْعَوْنَ أَشَدَّ الْعَذابِ ** (The day the Hour commences [it will be said]: 'Admit the clan of Pharaoh into the severest punishment!')" And it is also supported by His saying: ما دامَتِ السَّماواتُ وَالْأَرْضُ (as long as the heavens and the earth endure), meaning the heavens of the world (سموات الدنيا) and its earth, as is known.

And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Baqir and Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon them both) that the meaning is that what is intended by Paradise (الجنّة) and the Fire (النّار) in this Ayah is the guardianship (ولاية) of the Family of Muhammad (may Allah bless him and his family and grant them peace) and the guardianship (ولاية) of their enemies.

He said: Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) said: "The one ignorant of the science of exegesis (علم التفسير) claims that this exception (الاستثناء) from Allah is only for those who enter Paradise and the Fire, and that both groups will exit them, so they will remain empty, with no one in them. They have lied. He (peace be upon him) said: By Allah, the Blessed and Exalted, the people of Paradise, nor all the people of the Fire, will ever exit them. How can that be, when Allah the Almighty has said in His Book: ماكِثِينَ فِيهِ أَبَداً ** (abiding in it forever), and there is no exception (استثناء) in it."

And Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) said: "These two Ayahs concern those among the wretched (أهل الشقاوة) and the felicitous (السَّعادة) who are not destined for eternity (أهل الخلود)."

(109) فَلا تَكُ فِي مِرْيَةٍ ** (109) So do not be in doubt concerning doubt (في شك) مِمَّا يَعْبُدُ هؤُلاءِ ** concerning what these worship. after what has been revealed to you of these stories concerning the evil consequence of their worship of idols (الأوثان) and their exposure thereby to what afflicted their likes before them. (This is) a consolation (تسلية) for the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, a promise to Him of vengeance against them, and a threat to them. مَا يَعْبُدُونَ إِلَّا كَمَا يَعْبُدُ آبَاؤُهُمْ مِنْ قَبْلُ They do not worship except as their fathers worshipped before. meaning, their state in polytheism (الشرك) is like the state of their fathers, without any difference (تفاوت) between the two states, so there will descend upon them the like of what descended upon their fathers. (This is) a justification (تعليل) for the prohibition against doubt (المرية). وَإِنَّا لَمُوَفُّوهُمْ نَصِيبَهُمْ And We will indeed pay them in full their share their portion (حظّهم) of the punishment, like their fathers, غَيْرَ مَنْقُوصٍ without any deduction. without deficiency (بلا نقص).

(١١٠) وَلَقَدْ آتَيْنا مُوسَى الْكِتابَ فَاخْتُلِفَ فِيهِ (Certainly We gave Moses the Book, but they differed about it.) So a group believed in it and a group disbelieved in it, just as these people differed concerning the Quran. In al-Kafi, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): They differed just as this nation (الأمّة) differed concerning the Book, and they will differ concerning the Book which is with the Riser (القائم), which he brings to them, until some people among them deny it, so he will bring them forward and strike their necks. وَلَوْ لا كَلِمَةٌ سَبَقَتْ مِنْ رَبِّكَ (Had it not been for a word that went before from your Lord,) meaning the word of respite (الإِنظار) until the Day of Resurrection, لَقُضِيَ بَيْنَهُمْ (it would have certainly been decided between them.) by sending down what the one who follows falsehood (المبطل) deserves, so that he may be distinguished from the one who follows truth (المحقّ). وَإِنَّهُمْ And they, meaning the disbelievers (كفّار) among your people, لَفِي شَكٍّ مِنْهُ مُرِيبٍ (and they are indeed in a dubious doubt about it.) concerning the Quran, a dubious doubt (مُرِيبٍ) that causes suspicion (الريّبة).

(١١١) The Almighty's saying: وَإِنَّ كُلًّا ** And indeed all of the differing ones, the believers and the disbelievers, لَمَّا لَيُوَفِّيَنَّهُمْ رَبُّكَ أَعْمالَهُمْ. (Surely your Lord will pay them back fully for their deeds.) Al-Qummi said: [This refers to] on the Day of Resurrection. It was said: The lām (الّلام) in lammā (لَمَّا) is preparatory for an oath (توطئة للقسم), and the other [lām] is for emphasis (للتوكيد), or vice versa, and mā (ما) is extra (مزيدة) to separate them. Meaning: And indeed all of them, by Allah, your Lord will surely pay them back the recompense of their deeds. And it was recited: an (أن) with takhfif (التخفيف) instead of the heavy form (المثقلة), treating the lightened form as acting like the heavy form, considering its origin. And lammā (لَمَّا) with tashdid (التشديد), based on the premise that its origin is la-man mā (لَمَنْ مَا), meaning: for those (لِمَنِ الَّذِينَ) whom He pays back fully. And Ubayy recited: wa in kullun (وَإِنْ كُلٌّ) with raf' (الرّفع) and lammā (لَمَّا) with tashdid (التشديد), based on the premise that in (إنْ) is negative (نافية) and lammā (لَمَّا) means illā (إلا) (except). This is supported by the reading of al-Imkān (الإمكان) lammā (لَمَّا). إِنَّهُ بِما يَعْمَلُونَ خَبِيرٌ ** (Indeed, He is well-aware of what they do.) So nothing escapes Him, even if it is hidden.

(112) فَاسْتَقِمْ كَما أُمِرْتَ ** (112) So be steadfast, just as you have been commanded, upon the path (جادة) of truth, without deviating from it. And this (steadfastness) encompasses beliefs (العقائد) and deeds (الأعمال). وَمَنْ تابَ مَعَكَ ** and those who have repented with you, And let him be steadfast, (he) who has repented (مَنْ تابَ) from disbelief (الكفر) and believed مَعَكَ وَلا تَطْغَوْا ** (with you. And do not transgress), meaning: do not exceed the limits (حدود) of Allah. إِنَّهُ بِما تَعْمَلُونَ بَصِيرٌ ** (Indeed He is watchful of what you do). So He will reward you for it. In al-Jawami', narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding: فَاسْتَقِمْ كَما أُمِرْتَ ** (So be steadfast, just as you have been commanded). He said: Meaning, be needy (افتقر) toward Allah with sincerity of resolve (بصحة العزم). And from Ibn 'Abbas: No verse was revealed that was harder upon the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم than this verse. And for this reason, He said: "Hud, al-Waqi'ah, and their sisters have made me gray."

(١١٣) وَلا تَرْكَنُوا إِلَى الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا ** (113) And do not incline toward those who do wrong And do not incline toward them even the slightest inclination, for الرّكون (inclination) is the slight leaning. فَتَمَسَّكُمُ النَّارُ ** lest the Fire should touch you due to your inclination (ركون) toward them.

In al-Majma', narrated from them (peace be upon them): That الركون (inclination) is affection (المودّة), sincere advice (النصيحة), and obedience (الطاعة). And al-Qummi narrated the like of it.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): It is the man who comes to the ruler and loves his survival until he puts his hand into his purse (كيسه) and gives him [something]. And al-Ayyashi narrated from him (peace be upon him): Indeed, He did not make it eternal (خلوداً), but it will touch you, so do not incline toward them. وَما لَكُمْ مِنْ دُونِ اللهِ مِنْ أَوْلِياءَ ** and you have no guardians besides Allah meaning, no helpers (أنصار) who can prevent the punishment from you. ثُمَّ لا تُنْصَرُونَ ** then you will not be helped. Then Allah will not help you.

** وَأَقِمِ الصَّلاةَ طَرَفَيِ النَّهارِ وَزُلَفاً مِنَ اللَّيْلِ ** (114) And maintain the prayer at the two ends of the day and in the early hours of the night. (meaning) hours of the night close to the day, derived from أزلفه (He brought it near) when He brought it near (قرّبه), and it is the plural of زلفة.

In al-Tahdhib, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): Its two ends (طرفاه) are the Maghrib (sunset) and the Ghadah (dawn). وَزُلَفاً مِنَ اللَّيْلِ ** (and in the early hours of the night) refers to the prayer of al-Isha' al-Akhirah (the last evening prayer).

And al-Ayyashi narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): The like of it.

**إِنَّ الْحَسَناتِ يُذْهِبْنَ السَّيِّئاتِ ** Indeed good deeds annul evil deeds. (meaning) they expiate them.

And in the well-known Prophetic tradition (الحديث النّبويّ): Indeed, the prayer to the prayer is an expiation for what is between them, so long as the major sins (الكبائر) are avoided.

And in al-Amali, narrated from the Commander of the Faithful (peace be upon him): Indeed, Allah expiates an evil deed (سيئة) with every good deed (حسنة). Then he recited the verse.

And in al-Kafi and al-Ayyashi, narrated from al-Sadiq (peace be upon him) regarding this verse: It is the prayer of the believer during the night that removes the sin he committed during the day. And al-Qummi narrated: The like of it.

And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him), he said: The Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم said: "Four things, if they are found in a person, he will not perish before Allah thereafter, except for one who is already doomed (هالك). The servant intends a good deed (الحسنة) and performs it; but if he does not perform it, Allah writes for him one good deed due to the good intention (نيّة). And if he performs it, Allah writes for him ten. And he intends an evil deed (السيّئة) to perform it; but if he does not perform it, nothing is written against him. And if he performs it, he is granted a delay of seven hours. And the master of good deeds (صاحب الحسنات) says to the master of evil deeds (صاحب السيئات), who is the master of the left (shoulder): 'Do not rush, perhaps he will follow it up with a good deed that will erase it, for Allah, the Mighty and Majestic, says: إِنَّ الْحَسَناتِ يُذْهِبْنَ السَّيِّئاتِ ** (Indeed good deeds annul evil deeds),' or with seeking forgiveness (الاستغفار). If he says: 'I seek forgiveness from Allah, besides Whom there is no god, the Knower of the Unseen and the Visible, the All-mighty, the All-wise, the All-forgiving, the All-merciful, the Possessor of Majesty and Generosity (ذو الجلال والإكرام), and I repent to Him (وأتوب إليه),' nothing is written against him. But if seven hours pass and he does not follow it up with a good deed or seeking forgiveness, the master of good deeds says to the master of evil deeds: 'Write it down against the wretched, deprived one (الشّقيّ المحروم).'"

And in al-Majma' and al-Ayyashi, narrated from him (peace be upon him): Know that there is nothing more harmful to well-being (عافية) nor quicker in bringing regret (ندامةً) than sin (الخطيئة). And there is nothing more persistent in seeking, nor quicker in overtaking sin, than the good deed (الحسنة). Indeed, it overtakes the great, ancient sin that is forgotten by its perpetrator, and it removes it, nullifies it, and takes it away after it had been established. And that is the saying of the Glorified One: **إِنَّ الْحَسَناتِ يُذْهِبْنَ السَّيِّئاتِ ذلِكَ ذِكْرى لِلذَّاكِرِينَ ** (Indeed good deeds annul evil deeds. That is a reminder for those who remember.)

And narrated from one of the two (Imams) عليهم السلام that Ali (peace be upon him) said: I heard my beloved, the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم, saying: "The most hopeful verse in the Book of Allah is أَقِمِ الصَّلاةَ طَرَفَيِ النَّهارِ (And maintain the prayer at the two ends of the day)." And He recited the entire verse and said: "O Ali, by Him Who sent me with the Truth as a bearer of good news and a warner, when one of you stands for his ablution (وضوئه), the sins fall away from his limbs. Then, when he faces Allah with his heart and his face, he does not turn away while any of his sins remain upon him, just as the day his mother gave birth to him. If he commits something between the two prayers, he receives the like of that (expiation)," until He counted the five prayers. Then He said: "O Ali, the likeness of the five prayers for my Ummah is like a flowing river at the door of one of them. What does one of them think if there is dirt (دون) on his body, and then he bathes in that river five times? Would any dirt (درن) remain on his body? Likewise, by Allah, are the five prayers for my Ummah."

ذلِكَ ** (That) It was said that it refers to His saying فَاسْتَقِمْ (So be upright) and what follows it. ذِكْرى لِلذَّاكِرِينَ ** (a reminder for those who remember) (meaning) an admonition (عظة) for those who take heed (المتّعظين).

(١١٥) وَاصْبِرْ ** (And be patient) upon acts of obedience (الطاعات) and abstaining from forbidden things (المنهيّات). فَإِنَّ اللهَ لا يُضِيعُ أَجْرَ الْمُحْسِنِينَ ** (for Allah does not waste the reward of the virtuous.) He shifted from the implicit pronoun (المضمر) so that it might serve as a proof (كالبرهان) for the intended meaning (المقصُود).

(١١٦) فَلَوْ لا كانَ ** (Why were there not) meaning: Why not (فهلّا) مِنَ الْقُرُونِ مِنْ قَبْلِكُمْ أُولُوا بَقِيَّةٍ ** (among the generations before you men of remaining virtue) of sound judgment (الرأي), intellect (العقل), and merit (الفضل). And it was only named بَقِيَّةٍ ** (remaining virtue) because a man keeps (يستبقي) for himself the best of what he brings forth. From this, it is said: "So-and-so is from the بقيّة of the people," meaning: from their best (خيارهم). And their saying: "In the corners (الزَّوايا) are hidden things (خنايا), and among men are remnants (بقايا)." يَنْهَوْنَ عَنِ الْفَسادِ فِي الْأَرْضِ إِلَّا قَلِيلاً مِمَّنْ أَنْجَيْنا مِنْهُمْ ** (who would forbid corruption in the earth, except a few of those whom We delivered from among them.) But a few of them We delivered because they forbade corruption. وَاتَّبَعَ الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا مَا أُتْرِفُوا فِيهِ (But those who were wrongdoers followed what they were given to enjoy therein) meaning: what they were granted of pleasures (الشّهوات). By الَّذِينَ ظَلَمُوا (those who were wrongdoers), He meant those who abandoned forbidding evil (تاركي النّهي عن المنكرات). That is, they followed what they were accustomed to of luxury (التنعّم) and seeking the means of a pleasant life (العيش الهَنيء), and they rejected what was beyond that. وَكانُوا مُجْرِمِينَ ** (and they were guilty.) It is as if He intended to explain the reason (سَبَب) for the eradication (اسْتيصال) of the previous nations, which was the spread of injustice (الظلم) among them, their following of desires (الهوى), and their abandonment of forbidding evil (النّهي عن المنكرات).

(١١٧) وَما كانَ رَبُّكَ لِيُهْلِكَ الْقُرى بِظُلْمٍ ** (117) Your Lord would never destroy the towns unjustly, from Him to them, or from them to themselves, such as polytheism (شرك) and disobedience (معصية), وَأَهْلُها مُصْلِحُونَ ** (while their inhabitants are righteous) among themselves. In al-Majma', narrated from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله وسلم: وَأَهْلُها مُصْلِحُونَ ** (while their inhabitants are righteous) means that some of them deal justly with others. I say: And that is due to the abundance of His mercy and His tolerance regarding His own rights, but not regarding the rights of His servants. And for this reason, it was said: Sovereignty (الملك) remains with disbelief (الكفر), but does not remain with injustice (الظلم).

(١١٨) وَلَوْ شاءَ رَبُّكَ لَجَعَلَ النَّاسَ أُمَّةً واحِدَةً ** (118) Had your Lord willed, He would have made mankind one single nation. (All of them being Muslims). Al-Qummi (said): Meaning: upon one single doctrine (مذهب واحِد). وَلا يَزالُونَ مُخْتَلِفِينَ ** and they will never cease to differ, Some of them chose the truth and some of them chose falsehood (الباطل). You hardly find two who agree absolutely.

(١١٩) إِلَّا مَنْ رَحِمَ رَبُّكَ ** (119) save those on whom your Lord has mercy. Except for people whom Allah guided and treated with kindness (لُطْف), so they agreed upon the religion of truth. وَلِذلِكَ خَلَقَهُمْ (And for that He created them). It is said: If the pronoun (الضَّمِير) in خَلَقَهُمْ (He created them) refers to the people (الناس), then the reference (الإِشارة) is to the disagreement (الإِختلاف), and the lām (اللام) is for the consequence (العاقبة), or it refers to both the disagreement and the mercy (الرحمة) together. But if the pronoun refers to مَنْ (those), then the reference is to the mercy.

In al-Kafi, al-Ayyashi, and al-Ilal, narrated from Imam al-Sadiq (peace be upon him): They were أُمَّةً واحِدَةً ** (one nation), so Allah sent the Prophets to establish the proof (الحُجَّة) against them. And in al-Tawhid, narrated from him (peace be upon him): خَلَقَهُمْ ** (He created them) so that they may do what makes them deserving of His mercy, and then He will have mercy on them. And in al-Kafi, narrated from him (peace be upon him) regarding this Ayah: People disagree concerning the correctness of the saying (إِصَابَة القَوْل). And all of them are doomed إِلَّا مَنْ رَحِمَ رَبُّكَ ** (save those on whom your Lord has mercy), and they are our followers (شِيعَتُنَا). And for His mercy خَلَقَهُمْ ** (He created them), and that is His saying فَلِذلِكَ خَلَقَهُمْ ** (And for that He created them), meaning: for the obedience of the Imam (الإِمَام). And al-Qummi narrated from Imam al-Baqir (peace be upon him) who said: وَلا يَزالُونَ مُخْتَلِفِينَ ** (And they will not cease to differ) in religion إِلَّا مَنْ رَحِمَ رَبُّكَ ** (save those on whom your Lord has mercy), meaning the Family of Muhammad (may the blessings of Allah be upon them and their progeny) and their followers. Allah says: وَلِذلِكَ خَلَقَهُمْ ** (And for that He created them), meaning the people of mercy (أَهْل رَحْمَة) who do not differ in religion. And al-Ayyashi narrated from Imam al-Sajjad (peace be upon him): Regarding His saying وَلا يَزالُونَ مُخْتَلِفِينَ ** (And they will not cease to differ), He meant those of this nation who oppose us, and all of them oppose one another in their religion. As for His saying إِلَّا مَنْ رَحِمَ رَبُّكَ وَلِذلِكَ خَلَقَهُمْ ** (save those on whom your Lord has mercy. And for that He created them), those are our allies (أَوْلِيَاؤُنَا) among the believers. وَلِذلِكَ خَلَقَهُمْ ** (And for that He created them) from the pure clay (الطِّينَة الطَّيِّبَة)—the narration continues.

وَتَمَّتْ كَلِمَةُ رَبِّكَ ** And the word of your Lord has been fulfilled: And that is His saying: لَأَمْلَأَنَّ جَهَنَّمَ مِنَ الْجِنَّةِ وَالنَّاسِ ** (I will surely fill hell with jinn and humans) from all those who disobey Him among both of them. Al-Qummi (said): And they are those for whom wretchedness (الشَّقَاء) preceded, so the saying became due upon them that they were created for the Fire. And they are الَّذِينَ حَقَّتْ عَلَيْهِمْ كَلِمَتُ رَبِّكَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ. (those against whom the word of your Lord has become due; they will not believe.)

(١٢٠) وَكُلًّا نَقُصُّ عَلَيْكَ مِنْ أَنْباءِ الرُّسُلِ ** (120) And all that We relate to you of the accounts of the apostles We inform you of it ما نُثَبِّتُ بِهِ فُؤادَكَ ** is that by which We strengthen your heart. This is an alert to the intended purpose of the narration, which is the increase of his certainty (يَقِينِهِ), the tranquility of his heart (قَلْبِهِ), and the steadfastness of his soul (نَفْسِهِ) upon fulfilling the message (أَدَاءِ الرِّسَالَة) and enduring harm (احْتِمَال الْأَذَى) وَجاءَكَ فِي هذِهِ ** In these [accounts] there has come to you the accounts (الْأَنْبَاء) narrated to you الْحَقُ ** the truth, that which is true وَمَوْعِظَةٌ وَذِكْرى لِلْمُؤْمِنِينَ. and an admonition and a reminder for the faithful.

(١٢١) وَقُلْ لِلَّذِينَ لا يُؤْمِنُونَ اعْمَلُوا عَلى مَكانَتِكُمْ ** (121) And say to those who do not have faith, 'Act according to your ability; (meaning) your state (حالكم) which you are upon. إِنَّا عامِلُونَ (We are acting) according to Our state. Al-Qummi (said): meaning, We shall punish you.

(١٢٢) وَانْتَظِرُوا ** and wait. (Wait) for the misfortunes (الدّوائر) to befall us. إِنَّا مُنْتَظِرُونَ ** (We are waiting) that something similar to what descended upon your likes should descend upon you.

(١٢٣) وَلِلَّهِ غَيْبُ السَّماواتِ وَالْأَرْضِ ** (123) To Allah belongs the unseen of the heavens and the earth, not to anyone else. وَإِلَيْهِ يُرْجَعُ الْأَمْرُ كُلُّهُ ** and to Him all matters are referred. not to anyone else. And it was recited with the dammah on the yā’ (i.e., يُرْجَعُ). فَاعْبُدْهُ وَتَوَكَّلْ عَلَيْهِ ** So worship Him and rely on Him, for He is sufficient for you. وَما رَبُّكَ بِغافِلٍ عَمَّا تَعْمَلُونَ ** and your Lord is not oblivious of what you do. you and they, and He will recompense everyone according to what he deserves. And it was recited with the yā’ (i.e., يَعْمَلُونَ).

In Thawab al-A'mal, narrated from al-Baqir (peace be upon him): "Whoever recites Surah Hud every Friday, Allah will raise him on the Day of Resurrection in the company (زمرة) of the Prophets, and no sin that he committed will be known for him on the Day of Resurrection." And praise belongs to Allah.
